glossary.proposed_definition: A person who interacts with the nopCommerce store, including registered users with accounts and guest/anonymous users who browse and purchase without registration.

A merchandise item offered for sale in the store, which may be physical goods, digital downloads, services, or gift cards. Products can have attributes (size, color), variants (SKU combinations), and specifications (technical details).
A temporary container in the system that holds products the customer has selected for purchase, with configurable quantities and attributes, allowing review and modification before checkout.
The multi-step or single-page process through which a customer provides billing/shipping addresses, selects shipping and payment methods, and finalizes an order for purchase.

A complete transaction record representing a customer's purchase, including products ordered, quantities, pricing, billing/shipping addresses, payment method, shipping method, and associated statuses (order status, payment status, shipping status).

A persistent list of products that a registered customer saves for future reference, deferred purchasing, or sharing with others, with capabilities to modify quantities, move items to cart, and share via unique URL.

A hierarchical organizational unit for grouping related products (e.g., Computers, Electronics, Apparel), supporting parent-child relationships (subcategories) and used for storefront navigation and product filtering.
A brand or company name associated with products, used as a filtering criterion and organizational structure for product discovery and navigation on catalog pages.
A unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a product (or product attribute combination) for internal inventory tracking, ordering, and differentiation from other products.

Customizable properties of a product (e.g., size, color, material) that customers select during purchase, potentially affecting price and weight, and creating distinct product variants (SKU combinations).

The monetary unit (e.g., US Dollar, Euro) used to display and process pricing throughout the store, with support for multi-currency configuration where customers select their preferred display currency and prices are converted using configured exchange rates.

An alphanumeric code entered by customers in the shopping cart to apply promotional discounts to their order, supporting various discount types (percentage, fixed amount), usage limitations, and scheduling.

A prepaid card product (virtual or physical) with a unique coupon code and monetary value that customers can purchase and redeem as payment for future orders, supporting balance tracking and transfers.

A delivery option available to the customer (e.g., Ground, Next Day Air, Pickup in Store) with associated cost, description, and delivery timeline, calculated based on destination, products, and configured rates.

A financial mechanism for settling an order's cost (e.g., Credit Card, Check/Money Order, PayPal, Stripe), with configurable restrictions by country, order amount, and transaction mode (authorize only vs. capture).
The customer's address information used for invoice and payment processing, including name, email, country, state, city, street address, postal code, and phone number.
The address to which the ordered products will be physically delivered, captured during checkout and potentially different from the billing address.
The current fulfillment state of an order, with values: Pending (initial state), Processing (payment received), Complete (fulfilled and shipped), or Cancelled.

The current financial state of an order's payment transaction, with values: Pending (awaiting payment), Authorized (approved but not captured), Paid (captured), Partially Refunded, Refunded, or Voided.
The current fulfillment state of an order's physical delivery, with values: Not yet shipped, Partially shipped, Shipped (in transit), or Delivered (destination received).

Customer-submitted feedback on a product including a title, text body, and 1-5 star rating, potentially supporting custom review types (Quality, Value, Price) and approval workflow before public display.
A keyword-based retrieval system that searches product names, descriptions, SKUs, tags, and other fields; supporting autocomplete, filtering, sorting, and result pagination.

The process by which a customer creates a persistent account in the system, providing personal details (name, email, password) and optionally selecting preferences (newsletter subscription, gender, date of birth), supporting multiple registration methods (standard, email validation required, admin approval required).

The security mechanism by which a registered customer verifies their identity through email/username and password, with support for 'Remember Me' persistent login, lockout protection, and optional external authentication methods (social login).

The ability for an unregistered customer to complete a purchase by providing billing/shipping information without creating a persistent account, available when 'Anonymous checkout allowed' is enabled.
A monetary charge calculated on orders based on product tax categories, customer address (country/state), and configured tax rules, displayed as a separate line item in order totals.
A loyalty currency awarded to customers for purchases and other actions, accumulating in their account and optionally redeemable as payment or displayed in order totals.

The numerical count of a product unit selected by a customer for purchase, subject to validation rules including minimum/maximum cart quantities, allowed quantities (if restricted list), and stock availability.
Technical or descriptive properties of a product (e.g., screen size, CPU type, RAM) displayed in a structured table on product details pages, often filterable on category/search result pages.
Configurable dynamic fields presented during checkout (e.g., gift wrapping option) that apply to the entire order, potentially with conditional display rules and price/weight adjustments.

Static content blocks in the system (managed in Content management → Topics) used for informational pages such as FAQ, Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, About Us, shipping information, etc., supporting rich text, SEO settings, and conditional display.
A hierarchical path display (Home > Category > Subcategory > Product) at the top of pages showing the user's location within the site structure, with clickable links enabling backward navigation.
A feature allowing customers to select multiple products and view them side-by-side in a tabular format, comparing specification attributes, prices, and descriptions to aid purchasing decisions.
Keyword labels assigned to products (comma-separated) used for narrow search, discovery, and navigation, displayed in a tag cloud with size proportional to usage frequency.

Content published in the blog section including a title, body overview (excerpt), full body (rich text), publication date, tags, and optional comments section, supporting scheduling, multi-language, and RSS feed.
A time-sensitive announcement or update published in the news section, including title, short description, full description, and publication date, supporting scheduling and homepage display.

features.business_story: As a customer, I want to select my preferred currency and see all prices in that currency, So that I can understand costs in my local monetary system
As a customer, I want to register for an account, log in securely, and manage my profile, So that I can maintain a persistent account with personalized features
As a customer, I want to save products I like to a wishlist for later purchase consideration and sharing, So that I can organize products of interest and share my preferences with others
As a customer, I want to add products to a cart, modify quantities, view my selections before checkout, and apply discounts, So that I can control my purchase and ensure accuracy before payment
As a customer, I want to search for products using keywords, apply filters, and discover items efficiently, So that I can quickly find products that meet my needs
sub_features: Product sorting (by price, name, date, rating)

features.business_story: As a customer, I want to browse products organized by categories and manufacturers with filters and multiple view options, So that I can easily navigate the product catalog and find items I want
As a customer, I want to view detailed product information including images, descriptions, prices, specifications, and ratings, So that I can make informed purchasing decisions
As a store owner, I want to display promotional banners, featured products, and informational content on the homepage, So that I can showcase products and engage customers with relevant information
As a customer, I want to access support through multiple channels (contact form, forums, documentation) and get help when needed, So that my questions and concerns are addressed promptly
As a customer, I want to enter and confirm my billing and shipping addresses during checkout with form validation, So that my order is delivered to the correct location
As a customer, I want to select a shipping method with transparent cost display and clear descriptions, So that I can choose the delivery option that best meets my needs
As a customer, I want to select a payment method with clear descriptions and transparent information, So that I can proceed with payment using my preferred method
As a customer, I want to securely enter payment information for my selected payment method, So that I can complete the transaction with confidence
As a customer, I want to review all order details before finalizing my purchase, So that I can ensure accuracy and make any necessary corrections
As a guest customer, I want to complete a purchase without creating an account, So that I can quickly check out if I don't intend to make future purchases with the store
As a new customer, I want to register for an account with personal information, So that I can maintain an account for future shopping and access account features
As a returning customer, I want to log in securely and access my account, So that I can view my order history, manage my profile, and access saved information
As a customer, I want to view my order history, track order status, and access order details at any time, So that I can monitor my purchases and have a record of transactions
As a customer, I want to view product reviews and ratings from other customers and submit my own reviews, So that I can make informed purchasing decisions and share my experience
As a customer or search engine, I want to view a comprehensive sitemap of the website, So that I can navigate the site easily and ensure all content is discoverable
As a customer, I want to stay informed about store news, updates, and announcements, So that I don't miss important information or promotional updates
As a customer or store owner, I want to read blog posts and interact with content through comments, So that I can stay engaged with the store's editorial content
As a customer, I want to compare multiple products side-by-side, So that I can make well-informed purchasing decisions by evaluating product differences
As a customer, I want to proceed from my shopping cart to the checkout process, So that I can complete my purchase
sub_features: Checkout validation (terms of service, minimum order)

features.business_story: As a store owner, I want to require customers to accept terms of service before checkout, So that I ensure legal compliance and customers acknowledge important policies
sub_features: Link to terms of service content

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - User Registration with Valid Credentials
scenarios.given: A new customer is on the registration page and all required fields are visible with proper validation rules
scenarios.when: the customer enters valid first name, last name, email, password meeting complexity requirements (minimum 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit, 1 special char), confirms password, and clicks Register
scenarios.then: the account is created, customer is logged in automatically, and redirected to the home page with their name displayed in the header
scenarios.and: the system assigns the customer to the 'Registered' customer role and allows access to wishlist and order history features

scenarios.scenario_name: Negative - Login with Invalid Credentials
scenarios.given: a registered customer exists with email 'john@example.com' and password 'SecurePass123!'
scenarios.when: another user enters 'john@example.com' with incorrect password 'WrongPassword123!' and clicks Login
scenarios.then: the system displays error message 'The credentials provided are incorrect' without revealing whether the email exists
scenarios.and: the login attempt counter increments; after 5 failed attempts (configurable), the account locks for 30 minutes (configurable lockout duration)

scenarios.scenario_name: Exception - Forgot Password Recovery Flow
scenarios.given: a registered customer with email 'jane@example.com' forgot their password
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Forgot password?' on the login page, enters 'jane@example.com', and submits the recovery form
scenarios.then: the system sends a password recovery email with a unique time-limited reset link valid for 7 days (configurable)
scenarios.and: customer clicks the link, enters a new password meeting complexity rules, confirms it, and successfully logs in with the new password

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Add Product to Cart and Checkout
scenarios.given: a customer is viewing a product details page for 'Samsung 32" Monitor' priced at $299.99 with 5 units in stock
scenarios.when: the customer selects quantity 2, clicks 'Add to cart', and the system adds the product to the cart
scenarios.then: the cart header icon updates showing 2 items, the shopping cart page displays the product with SKU, image, unit price ($299.99), quantity (2), and line total ($599.98)
scenarios.and: the order summary shows sub-total ($599.98), and the 'Checkout' button becomes enabled

scenarios.scenario_name: Edge Case - Update Cart Quantity Beyond Available Stock
scenarios.given: a customer has added 3 units of 'USB Cable' to cart, but only 5 total units exist in inventory
scenarios.when: the customer changes quantity in cart to 8 units and clicks 'Update shopping cart'
scenarios.then: the system validates stock availability and displays validation error: 'Quantity exceeds available stock. Maximum available: 5 units'
scenarios.and: the quantity field reverts to the previous valid quantity (3), and cart totals are not recalculated until a valid quantity is entered

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Apply Valid Coupon Code
scenarios.given: a customer has $299.98 in their cart, and a valid coupon code 'SAVE20' exists offering 20% discount on orders over $200
scenarios.when: the customer enters 'SAVE20' in the Discount code field and clicks 'Apply coupon'
scenarios.then: the discount is applied, the order summary updates showing Discount: -$59.99, and the new Grand Total becomes $239.99
scenarios.and: the coupon code is removed from the input field, and a success message displays 'Coupon code was successfully applied'

scenarios.scenario_name: Negative - Apply Expired or Invalid Coupon
scenarios.given: a customer has $299.98 in their cart, and coupon 'EXPIRED2024' was valid until 12/31/2024 (now expired)
scenarios.when: the customer enters 'EXPIRED2024' in the Discount code field and clicks 'Apply coupon'
scenarios.then: the system validates the coupon and displays error: 'The coupon code was not found or has expired'
scenarios.and: no discount is applied, the order totals remain unchanged, and the coupon code input field is cleared

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Multi-Country Checkout with Address Validation
scenarios.given: a guest customer is on the Billing Address checkout step and enters: First Name 'Marie', Last Name 'Dupont', Email 'marie@example.fr', Country 'France', State 'Île-de-France', City 'Paris', Address '123 Rue de la Paix', Zip '75000'
scenarios.when: all fields are populated with valid data matching the selected country's format requirements, and customer clicks 'Continue'
scenarios.then: the system validates all mandatory fields are completed, email format is correct, and zip code matches France's format (5 digits or alphanumeric)
scenarios.and: the billing address is saved in session, and the checkout advances to the Shipping Address step (or 'Ship to same address' is pre-selected)

scenarios.scenario_name: Edge Case - Incomplete Mandatory Address Fields
scenarios.given: a customer is on the Billing Address step and enters: First Name 'John', Last Name 'Doe', Country 'United States', but leaves City and State empty
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Continue' without populating the mandatory City and State fields
scenarios.then: the system displays inline validation errors next to the empty City and State fields: 'Field is required'
scenarios.and: the form does not submit, the customer remains on the Billing Address step, and can correct the errors and retry

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Select Shipping Method with Cost Transparency
scenarios.given: a customer has completed address entry and is on the Shipping Method step with three options: 'Ground - $9.99 (5-7 business days)', 'Next Day Air - $29.99', '2-Day Air - $19.99'
scenarios.when: the customer selects '2-Day Air - $19.99' (by default, Ground is pre-selected), and clicks 'Continue'
scenarios.then: the system validates that a shipping method is selected and advances to the Payment Method step
scenarios.and: the order totals are updated to include the shipping cost of $19.99, and the shipping method selection is persisted in the checkout session

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Select Credit Card as Payment Method
scenarios.given: a customer is on the Payment Method step with two configured options: 'Credit Card' and 'Check / Money Order', and Credit Card is pre-selected by default
scenarios.when: the customer confirms the pre-selected Credit Card option and clicks 'Continue'
scenarios.then: the system advances to the Payment Information step with fields for credit card type, cardholder name, card number, expiration date, and CVV
scenarios.and: the selected payment method (Credit Card) is displayed in the order review section for verification

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Enter Valid Credit Card Details and Validate
scenarios.given: a customer is on the Payment Information step and enters: Card Type 'Visa', Cardholder Name 'John Doe', Card Number '4532123456789010', Expiration '12/2027', CVV '123'
scenarios.when: all credit card fields are completed with valid data matching card type validation rules (Visa starts with 4, CVV is 3 digits), and customer clicks 'Continue'
scenarios.then: the system validates the card number passes the Luhn algorithm, expiration date is not in the past, and all fields are non-empty
scenarios.and: the payment information is encrypted and temporarily held in the session, and checkout advances to the Order Confirmation step (without storing full card number per PCI DSS)

scenarios.scenario_name: Negative - Invalid Credit Card Number (Fails Luhn Check)
scenarios.given: a customer enters credit card details with Card Number '4532123456789999' (invalid Luhn checksum), expiration 12/2027, CVV 123
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Continue' to submit the payment form
scenarios.then: the system performs client-side Luhn validation and displays error: 'The credit card number is invalid'
scenarios.and: the form submission is blocked, customer remains on Payment Information step, and can correct the card number and retry

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Review and Confirm Order with All Details
scenarios.given: a customer reaches the Confirm Order step with: 2x Samsung Monitor ($599.98), Ground Shipping ($9.99), Tax ($45.00), Discount -$59.99, Grand Total ($594.98), and all billing/shipping/payment details entered
scenarios.when: the customer reviews the complete order summary including billing address, shipping address, shipping method, payment method, product details (SKU, images, prices, quantities), and order totals
scenarios.then: all information is correctly displayed, and customer clicks 'Confirm Order' to finalize the purchase
scenarios.and: the system performs final validation on all order components (address completeness, shipping validity, payment method validity, stock availability, minimum order amount, terms agreement), creates the order, and displays Order Completed page with order number and status

scenarios.scenario_name: Negative - Proceed to Checkout Without Terms Agreement
scenarios.given: Terms of Service agreement is required on the Shopping Cart page (Order settings configured), and customer has items in cart but Terms checkbox is unchecked
scenarios.when: the customer clicks the 'Checkout' button without checking the Terms of Service agreement checkbox
scenarios.then: the system displays validation error: 'Please accept the terms of service' and blocks checkout from proceeding
scenarios.and: the Checkout button remains disabled until the customer checks the agreement checkbox

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Complete Purchase as Guest Without Account Creation
scenarios.given: 'Anonymous checkout allowed' is enabled in Order settings, and a customer with items in cart reaches the checkout login page
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Checkout as Guest' button instead of Register or Login
scenarios.then: the checkout process bypasses login/registration and proceeds directly to the Billing Address step
scenarios.and: the customer completes checkout by entering billing address, shipping address, selecting shipping/payment methods, and confirming the order without creating a persistent account

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Search Product with Keyword and Filter Results
scenarios.given: the search bar is visible in the store header, and 50 products exist matching keyword 'laptop'
scenarios.when: a customer enters 'laptop' in the search box (minimum 3 characters, autocomplete suggests matching products), and clicks Search or presses Enter
scenarios.then: the search results page displays 12 matching products (default page size), organized in grid view, showing product image, name, price, rating, and 'Add to cart'/'Add to wishlist' buttons
scenarios.and: sorting options (Position, Name, Price, Date) and page size selection (6, 12, 24 items) are available at the top of results; customer can sort by price or change page size without reloading

scenarios.scenario_name: Edge Case - Search with Minimum Character Length Validation
scenarios.given: search term minimum length is configured to 3 characters in Catalog settings
scenarios.when: a customer enters 'PC' (2 characters) in the search box and attempts to search
scenarios.then: the system displays validation message: 'Minimum search term length is 3 characters' and does not execute the search
scenarios.and: the customer must enter at least 3 characters ('PCX', 'PCs', etc.) before the search can proceed

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - View Comprehensive Product Details with Multiple Attributes
scenarios.given: a customer is viewing the product details page for 'Gaming Laptop - Model X1' with multiple variants (color: Black/Silver, storage: 256GB/512GB)
scenarios.when: the customer views the product page with: product name (H1), image gallery with zoom, full description, SKU (SKU-LAPTOP-X1), price ($1,299.99), old price ($1,499.99, strikethrough), availability status (In Stock), specification attributes table (CPU: Intel i7, RAM: 16GB), product attribute dropdowns (Color, Storage) with price adjustments, quantity selector, 'Add to cart'/'Add to wishlist'/'Add to compare' buttons, product ratings (4.5 stars from 12 reviews), and related/cross-sell products
scenarios.then: all information is correctly formatted and displayed; customer can select attributes (Black + 512GB = $1,399.99 adjusted price) and quantity before adding to cart
scenarios.and: breadcrumb navigation shows 'Home > Computers > Laptops > Gaming Laptop - Model X1' enabling backward navigation

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Compare Multiple Products Side-by-Side
scenarios.given: a customer is comparing 3 laptops and has added them to the compare list via 'Add to compare list' button on product pages
scenarios.when: the customer navigates to the Compare Products page, viewing a side-by-side table with columns for each product
scenarios.then: the comparison displays: product images, product names (as links), prices, short/full descriptions (if enabled), and all specification attributes (CPU, RAM, Storage, Screen Size, Weight) in rows for easy side-by-side evaluation
scenarios.and: the customer can remove individual products from the comparison list, and the table updates dynamically

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Add Product to Wishlist and Share via URL
scenarios.given: a registered customer is viewing a product details page for a desired item
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Add to wishlist' button, the product is added to their wishlist with default quantity 1
scenarios.then: the wishlist icon in the header updates showing increased item count; the wishlist page displays the product with SKU, image, name, unit price, editable quantity, and line total
scenarios.and: the customer can copy the unique wishlist URL and share it with friends; anyone visiting the URL can view the wishlist contents without needing account login

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Submit Product Review with Rating
scenarios.given: a registered customer has purchased the product 'Wireless Headphones' and 'Allow anonymous users to write product reviews' is disabled, requiring purchase verification
scenarios.when: the customer navigates to the product page, clicks 'Add your review', and submits: Review Title 'Great Sound Quality', Review Text 'Excellent sound with long battery life', and 5-star rating
scenarios.then: the review is submitted and, if 'Product reviews must be approved' is enabled, moves to pending approval state; store owner receives notification of the new review
scenarios.and: once approved, the review appears on the product page with reviewer name (optional), date, 5-star rating, and review text; the overall product rating (5/5 stars) is updated

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - View Order Details and Generate Invoice
scenarios.given: a registered customer has completed a purchase (Order #12345) and is viewing the Order Details page from My Account → Orders
scenarios.when: the customer views order details showing: Order Number (12345), Order Date (4/4/2026 14:30), Order Status (Processing), Payment Status (Paid), Shipping Status (Shipped), billing/shipping addresses, payment method (Visa **** 1234), shipping method (Ground - $9.99), product table (SKU, image, name, price, quantity, total), order totals, and action buttons
scenarios.then: all information is accurately displayed; customer can click 'Print' to open printer-friendly version or 'PDF Invoice' to download invoice as PDF with store logo, order details, and itemized breakdown
scenarios.and: customer can also click 'Re-order' to add all items from this order back to their shopping cart with current pricing/stock validated

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Navigate Site Using Sitemap
scenarios.given: a customer accesses the Sitemap page from the footer or directly via /sitemap
scenarios.when: the sitemap displays organized sections: General (Home, Search, News, Blog, Contact Us, My Account), Categories (with hierarchical structure), and Manufacturers
scenarios.then: all entries are clickable hyperlinks; customer can click 'Computers' to navigate to that category page, or 'Apple' to see all Apple brand products
scenarios.and: an XML sitemap (sitemap.xml) is also available for search engines for SEO indexing; the system ensures all published categories and manufacturers are included

scenarios.scenario_name: Happy Path - Read Blog Post and Subscribe to RSS Feed
scenarios.given: a customer visits the Blog page showing recent blog posts: 'New Product Launch' (4/1/2026), 'Customer Success Story' (3/28/2026), with short descriptions visible
scenarios.when: the customer clicks 'Read more' on 'New Product Launch', views the full blog post with body content, publication date, tags ('announcement', 'products'), and comments section showing 3 approved comments
scenarios.then: the customer can read the full post and submit their own comment (if 'Allow guests to leave comments' is enabled) or subscribe to the RSS feed via the RSS icon to receive notifications of new posts
scenarios.and: the blog archive sidebar shows months (April 2026, March 2026) and customer can filter posts by month; tag cloud shows related tags for content discovery

findings.finding_title: Missing Audit Logging for Sensitive Actions
findings.what_is_missing: No requirements for audit logging of sensitive customer/business actions: customer password changes, address modifications, refund processing, admin discount applications, inventory adjustments
findings.where_expected: System-wide NFR or Security requirements section
findings.impact: No accountability trail for fraudulent transactions; refund abuse undetected; admin malice impossible to track; compliance failures (SOC 2, PCI-DSS require audit logs)
findings.reasoning: Only REQ056-REQ057 mention 'security' in vague terms (lockout, encryption). No mention of audit logging anywhere in 153 requirements. Compliance standards require immutable logs of all privileged actions.
findings.recommendation: Add NFR: 'The system SHALL maintain immutable audit logs for: (1) All customer account changes (address, phone, email), (2) Payment transactions and refunds, (3) Admin discounts/overrides applied to orders, (4) Inventory adjustments > threshold, (5) Admin login/logout. Logs SHALL record timestamp, user ID, action, old value, new value, IP address, and be retained per data retention policy. Audit logs SHALL be read-only after creation.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Security Feature: Payment Card Data PCI Compliance
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements mention payment processing (REQ090-091) but do NOT address: (1) No direct storage of credit card data (PCI-DSS prohibits), (2) Use of tokenization or hosted payment pages, (3) PCI-DSS scope and certification, (4) Encryption of payment data in transit
findings.where_expected: Payment Processing feature or Security cross-cutting concern
findings.impact: Direct card storage = PCI-DSS violation; massive compliance penalties ($5K-$100K per incident); data breach liability catastrophic; merchant account termination
findings.reasoning: REQ090 validates card details but assumes cards are entered directly in nopCommerce form. This is PCI non-compliant. Must use payment gateway APIs or hosted payment pages.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'The system SHALL NOT store raw credit card data. Payment processing SHALL use one of: (1) Payment gateway API with tokenization (cards stored with payment processor), (2) Hosted payment page (payment processor hosts form, nopCommerce receives token only), (3) 3D Secure redirect. The system SHALL encrypt all payment-related data in transit (TLS 1.2+). PCI-DSS compliance SHALL be documented: (a) System deemed 'SAQ A' (outsource all card handling to gateway), OR (b) achieve annual PCI-DSS certification from qualified security assessor. Store shall provide proof of PCI compliance to customers upon request.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Payment Failure Recovery
findings.outcome_description: The system will automatically notify the customer and provide retry options when payment authorization fails
findings.triggering_scenario: Payment gateway rejects the transaction (insufficient funds, expired card, etc.)
findings.reasoning: Requirements F12-F13 (Payment Method/Information) define the collection and validation of payment details but have no specification of outcomes when payment fails. REQ090 validates card details but doesn't address what happens after authorization failure. No mention of: retry mechanisms, error messages beyond validation, customer notification, order hold, or resumption capability. This is critical for customer experience and recovery of lost sales.
findings.recommendation: Add specific requirement: When payment authorization fails, the system shall display detailed error message, offer retry option, save transaction attempt for audit, send failure notification email with recovery instructions, and optionally offer alternative payment methods. Consider implementing: 'Payment Retry' and 'Payment Failure Notification' as new sub-features.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Customer Registration Confirmation
findings.outcome_description: The system will send an email confirmation or verification message upon customer registration
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer submits the registration form (Feature F16, REQ051, REQ117-REQ125)
findings.reasoning: REQ051-REQ059 define the registration form fields, validation rules, and password complexity BUT do not explicitly state the outcome: 'the system sends a confirmation email.' REQ054 mentions 'Email validation required (confirmation email sent)' as a registration METHOD option, not as an outcome of registration itself. The requirement conflates configuration options with outcomes. No explicit requirement states the welcome email, verification email, or account created notification WILL be sent.
findings.recommendation: Create explicit outcome requirement: 'When registration is completed, the system shall send a confirmation email to the provided email address containing: account created confirmation, login instructions, account details summary, and a link to activate the account (if email validation is required). The welcome email SHALL be sent using the 'Customer.WelcomeMessage' or 'Customer.EmailValidationMessage' template depending on configuration.' Add as Feature F16 sub-feature or standalone.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Password Reset Email and Verification
findings.outcome_description: The system will send a password reset email with a unique, time-limited token to the customer
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer clicks 'Forgot Password' link (F17, REQ052, REQ054)
findings.reasoning: REQ054 provides extensive detail about the password recovery process: 'The system sends a password recovery email using the Customer.PasswordRecovery message template containing a unique, time-limited reset link.' However, this is DESCRIPTIVE of system behavior, not stated as a 'SHALL' outcome requirement. The requirement is written in passive/observational voice. No explicit requirement states the outcome: 'the system WILL send an email' or 'the system SHALL notify the customer.' The link validity duration is mentioned but no outcome if link expires.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite as explicit outcome: 'When a password recovery request is submitted, the system SHALL: (1) immediately send a password reset email to the registered email address containing a unique token, (2) expire the token after the configured validity period, (3) display error message if expired token is used, (4) send confirmation email when password is successfully changed.' Make this an explicit requirement vs. feature description.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Payment Gateway Decline/Failure Recovery
findings.scenario_text: Given a customer enters valid credit card details (Visa, not expired, correct CVV) and clicks 'Continue', When the payment gateway (e.g., Stripe) returns decline response 'Card declined - insufficient funds', Then the system should: (1) display clear error message 'Your card was declined. Please verify your card information or use a different payment method', (2) preserve entered payment data in session (except CVV/full card number per PCI), (3) provide 'Back' button to retry with same or different card, (4) NOT create an order record until payment authorizes, (5) NOT charge the customer's card if not already authorized
findings.reasoning: No scenario explicitly tests payment gateway decline/failure handling, which is critical for customer experience and financial integrity. REQ091 covers PCI compliance but not error handling for declined cards.
findings.recommendation: Add comprehensive exception scenarios for payment failures: declined cards, insufficient funds, fraud detection holds, temporary authorization failures, gateway timeouts. Document transaction state (pending vs. failed) and retry mechanisms. Test both authorize-only and authorize+capture flows.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Out-of-Stock Product Detection During Checkout
findings.scenario_text: Given a customer adds 'Limited Edition Widget' (5 units in stock) to cart on Monday, but 2 hours later other customers purchase the last 5 units, When the original customer proceeds to Confirm Order on Wednesday, Then the system should detect stock is now 0 and: (1) display error 'Product 'Limited Edition Widget' is no longer available' on the Confirm Order step, (2) allow customer to remove the item from order or cancel checkout, (3) NOT create the order record or charge payment, (4) suggest similar in-stock alternatives if available
findings.reasoning: Stock availability validation is mentioned (REQ098, REQ029), but no scenario explicitly addresses the race condition where inventory is depleted between cart addition and order confirmation. This prevents overselling and failed fulfillment.
findings.recommendation: Add exception scenarios for inventory depletion between cart and checkout completion. Test out-of-stock detection at order confirmation step. Test partial out-of-stock (customer added 5 units but only 2 remain). Test backorder handling if enabled.

findings.finding_title: Unpredictable Outcome: Race Condition in Stock Validation at Order Confirmation
findings.scenario: Multiple customers simultaneously add the same limited-stock product to cart and reach order confirmation. Between the time product is added to cart (stock checked in SC005) and order confirmation validation (REQ098), another customer completes their order and purchases the last units.
findings.predicted_outcome: System blocks order confirmation with message 'Item no longer in stock' and removes the unavailable items from the order
findings.realistic_alternative: System processes the order and then notifies the customer post-hoc that partial fulfillment will occur (backorder for insufficient units)
findings.absurd_alternative: System allows overselling by 200% and promises delivery in 3 months without notifying the customer
findings.reasoning: REQ030, REQ040, and REQ098 specify stock validation at cart add and confirmation, but requirements are silent on: (1) whether stock is RESERVED during checkout or only validated at confirmation, (2) the exact behavior when validation fails at confirmation (block order? auto-reduce quantity? split into partial/backorder?), (3) whether a customer lock/reservation mechanism exists to prevent race conditions. The gap between validation moments creates unpredictability in concurrent scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Explicitly define stock reservation strategy: (A) Reserve stock when item added to cart (locks units for 30 minutes), or (B) Validate at confirmation only (accept overselling risk). If validation fails at confirmation, specify exact behavior: block order with 'remove item/retry' options, or auto-convert to backorder with customer notification email. Define rollback behavior if payment succeeds but stock cannot be allocated.

findings.finding_title: Unpredictable Outcome: Tax Calculation Timing and Order Total Accuracy
findings.scenario: Customer's order totals display $99.00 on the cart page (calculated with estimated tax based on registered address). During checkout, customer enters a DIFFERENT shipping address in a state with different tax rate. Question: When is tax recalculated and shown to customer?
findings.predicted_outcome: System recalculates tax immediately when new shipping address is entered in the Shipping Address step, updates order totals to reflect new tax, and displays revised total before payment
findings.realistic_alternative: System does NOT recalculate tax until Payment Information step, showing original total on shipping step but surprising customer with higher total on payment page
findings.absurd_alternative: System calculates tax based on the store's headquarters location regardless of shipping address, showing inconsistent totals throughout checkout
findings.reasoning: REQ047 requires displaying order totals including tax, REQ062-REQ069 cover address entry during checkout, but the requirements NEVER specify: (1) WHEN tax is calculated (after each address entry? only on confirmation? during shipping method selection?), (2) WHETHER tax is shown/updated on intermediate checkout steps or only at confirmation, (3) HOW the system handles tax changes after customer commits to a shipping method, (4) WHAT happens if final calculated tax differs from estimated tax shown earlier. The interaction between address changes and tax recalculation is completely unspecified.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit requirement: 'The system SHALL recalculate tax in real-time at the following trigger points: (1) When billing address is entered/changed, (2) When shipping address is entered/changed and differs from billing address, (3) Before advancing from the Shipping Address step to Shipping Method. Tax SHALL be calculated based on: (A) the product tax category, (B) the shipping address (state/country), (C) the customer role (tax-exempt status). The updated order total SHALL be displayed immediately in the order totals summary. If tax increases substantially (>5% or >$X), display a notice: "Your tax was recalculated based on your new address: was $X, now $Y." Define tax calculation priority (shipping address vs. billing address) for tax jurisdiction determination.' Specify when and how tax is recalculated.

findings.finding_title: Undefined Reference: 'The User' - Customer vs Admin
findings.text_excerpt: The system shall allow users to select a currency from a dropdown menu located in the header/footer area. When a customer selects a different currency, all product prices are automatically recalculated.
findings.interpretation_a: 'Users' refers to storefront customers only (not admin users)
findings.interpretation_b: 'Users' could include both store customers AND administrators
findings.reasoning: Requirements use 'user', 'customer', and 'users' interchangeably. The glossary defines 'Customer' as persons who interact with nopCommerce store. But requirements don't always say 'customer'. This creates ambiguity in role-specific features—is 'user' always a storefront customer? What about admin users?
findings.recommendation: Consistently use 'Customer' for storefront users and 'Administrator' or 'Admin user' for backend users throughout all 153 requirements

findings.finding_title: Missing NFR: Data Retention and Purging Policy
findings.what_is_missing: No requirements specify data retention policies for customer data, orders, or system logs. No guidance on GDPR 'right to be forgotten' compliance or data purging procedures.
findings.where_expected: Cross-cutting NFR section or separate Privacy/Compliance feature
findings.impact: Legal liability under GDPR/CCPA; no enforcement of data minimization; customer data may be retained indefinitely, increasing breach risk
findings.reasoning: 150+ requirements cover functional features but no single requirement addresses retention. REQ051-REQ057 handle registration but not data lifecycle. Deletion must be explicit and testable.
findings.recommendation: Create NFR: 'The system SHALL support configurable data retention policies. Customer account data SHALL be purged N days after account deletion request. Order history SHALL be retained per country legal requirements (default 7 years). Access logs SHALL be purged after N days (configurable, default 90 days). Customer email addresses in marketing lists SHALL be removed within 48 hours of unsubscribe.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Error Handling Scenario: Invalid Payment Gateway Response
findings.what_is_missing: No requirements specify error handling for malformed or unexpected responses from payment gateways (e.g., payment processor returns invalid XML, timeout > 30s, or inconsistent response states)
findings.where_expected: REQ090-REQ091 (Payment Processing) should include error handling specifications
findings.impact: Checkout may hang indefinitely; payment may be charged without order creation; customer sees cryptic error message without recovery path. Double-charging risk.
findings.reasoning: REQ090 validates card details but doesn't address what happens when gateway responds with error/timeout. REQ091 mentions 'authorize only' vs 'capture' but no failure paths. Production e-commerce systems fail 0.5-2% of payment transactions; no specification for this.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When payment gateway returns error or times out (> 30 seconds), system SHALL: (1) Log full response/error for audit, (2) Display user-friendly message indicating retry, (3) Preserve order data in draft state, (4) Email customer with recovery link, (5) Send alert to admin if error rate exceeds threshold (>5% in 1 hour)'

findings.finding_title: Missing Completeness: Refund Processing Workflow Not Specified
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements define order display (REQ099-107) but nowhere specify customer refund requests, admin refund approval, payment reversal processing, or restocking logic after refund
findings.where_expected: Order Management or Customer Service feature section
findings.impact: Customers have no way to request refunds; no audit trail for refund decisions; inventory not restored after refund; no financial reconciliation of refunds vs. revenue
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention 'return requests' vaguely (REQ002) but no explicit refund workflow. Flo's analysis noted 'Order Return Management' as missing. This must include customer-initiated and admin-initiated refunds.
findings.recommendation: Create feature: 'Refund Management'. Must include: (1) Customer initiates return/refund request from My Account with reason, (2) Admin reviews request, approves/rejects, (3) Admin processes refund back to original payment method, (4) System marks order status as 'Refunded', updates payment status, (5) Inventory incremented for restocking, (6) Customer receives refund confirmation email with tracking. Support partial refunds, store credit alternatives, and refund deadlines (configurable, e.g., 30 days).

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Payment Processing Security Statements
findings.term: Credit Card Data Storage and Processing
findings.current_usage: REQ085 states 'the full card number should only be transmitted over HTTPS/SSL encrypted connections' while REQ091 states 'by default, credit card details are not stored in the nopCommerce database (except when using the Manual Credit Card method, where encrypted storage is optional)'
findings.proposed_definition: Credit card data handling follows PCI DSS compliance: (1) For most payment methods (PayPal, Stripe, Square, Authorize.Net): Card data is transmitted to payment gateway via HTTPS, never stored in nopCommerce database; (2) For Manual Credit Card method: Card data is optionally stored in encrypted form in nopCommerce database if configured; (3) In all cases, full card numbers are never logged or transmitted insecurely; CVV is never stored; tokenization is preferred over storage; access to stored card data is restricted and audited.
findings.reasoning: REQ085 emphasizes transmission security but doesn't address storage. REQ091 clarifies storage exceptions but doesn't fully explain payment gateway delegation. The two requirements create potential confusion: Does 'Manual Credit Card method' mean customers enter cards in nopCommerce forms (risky), or does it mean card info is processed manually by the merchant (after being transmitted securely)? The phrase 'encrypted storage is optional' could be misinterpreted as suggesting unencrypted storage is acceptable.
findings.recommendation: Create a comprehensive 'Payment Card Data Security Policy' glossary entry covering: (1) Payment method categories and data flows for each, (2) Encryption requirements for transmission and optional storage, (3) PCI DSS compliance obligations, (4) What data is stored where (gateway vs. local database), (5) Token-based payment recommendations, (6) CVV handling (never stored). Revise REQ085 and REQ091 to reference this entry and clarify the distinction between payment methods.

findings.finding_title: Checkout Process Requirements Lack Clear Step Sequencing
findings.business_story: As a developer, I want to understand the exact sequence and dependencies of checkout steps, So that I can implement the checkout flow correctly with proper data flow between steps
findings.reasoning: Checkout features span REQ037-098 with individual requirements for each step (address REQ061-069, shipping REQ070-076, payment REQ077-091, confirmation REQ092-098). While the individual steps are well-documented, the requirements lack: (1) explicit ordering (which step follows which), (2) data dependency mapping (what data from one step is used in the next), (3) branch conditions (e.g., when is shipping address skipped), (4) validation sequencing. The requirements assume readers understand 'standard' checkout flow but don't articulate it.
findings.recommendation: Create a Checkout Flow Diagram showing step sequence, data flow, and branch conditions. Explicitly define: (1) Standard flow: Address → Shipping → Payment → Confirmation, (2) Conditional branches: 'Ship to same address' skips shipping address step, (3) Guest checkout branch: No account creation/login required, (4) For each step, list required input data and output/state changes. Consider creating a separate 'Checkout Flow Architecture' requirement document.

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Order Return Management
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to initiate a return/exchange of a purchased product and track the return status, So that I can resolve issues with my purchases
findings.reasoning: REQ002 mentions 'return requests' as part of 'My Account' page ('view... return requests'), but there is NO requirement describing the complete return management feature. The requirements don't cover: (1) How customers initiate returns, (2) Return status tracking, (3) Refund processing, (4) Return shipping, (5) Return approval workflow. REQ002 only implies returns exist as a data display element without specifying the complete feature behavior. This is a critical gap for e-commerce platforms.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F23: Order Return Management' with business story and sub-features: (1) Return Initiation (customer submits return request with reason), (2) Return Approval (admin approves/denies), (3) Return Shipping (generate shipping label, track return), (4) Refund Processing, (5) Return Status Tracking. Add corresponding requirements for each sub-feature. This is critical for customer satisfaction in e-commerce.

findings.finding_title: Contradiction: Checkout Flow in REQ048 vs REQ050
findings.text_excerpt: REQ048: 'The checkout process flow: Billing Address → Shipping Address → Shipping Method → Payment Method → Payment Information → Confirm Order'. REQ050: 'When enabled, the checkout login page presents three options: (1) Checkout as Guest, (2) Register, (3) Returning Customer'.
findings.interpretation_a: Checkout begins directly with billing address (REQ048 flow)
findings.interpretation_b: Checkout begins with login/guest selection screen before billing address (REQ050)
findings.reasoning: REQ048 describes a specific ordered sequence of steps but doesn't mention the login screen that REQ050 says appears first. Which comes first: login screen or billing address? The descriptions contradict or are incomplete.
findings.recommendation: Clarify: 'Checkout process flow: 1) Authentication step (guest/register/login), 2) Billing Address, 3) Shipping Address...' OR 'If user is logged in, skip to Billing Address; else show login screen first'

findings.finding_title: Contradiction: Order Status Definitions Across Requirements
findings.text_excerpt: REQ099 (glossary): 'Order Status: Pending (initial state), Processing (payment received), Complete (fulfilled and shipped), or Cancelled'. REQ107: 'Order is automatically set to Complete when payment status is Paid and shipping status is Delivered (or Shipped if Complete order when delivered is disabled)'.
findings.interpretation_b: 'Processing' and 'Complete' have different meanings depending on context (glossary vs actual logic)
findings.reasoning: Glossary definition contradicts the actual logic in REQ107. Glossary says 'Complete = fulfilled and shipped' but REQ107 says 'Complete can mean Shipped (if setting disabled)'. What is the actual definition? When exactly is order Complete?
findings.recommendation: Unify definition: 'Order Status definitions: Pending (created, awaiting payment), Processing (payment received, preparing shipment), Shipped (in transit), Complete (delivered OR shipped for non-shipped products), Cancelled (customer or admin cancelled)'

findings.finding_title: Weasel Words: 'Should' vs 'Shall' in Security Requirements
findings.text_excerpt: REQ056: 'Store owners are advised to use a dedicated IP address with SSL certificate and ensure hosting meets PCI compliance requirements'. REQ091: 'Store owners are advised to use...SSL/HTTPS to encrypt data'
findings.interpretation_a: Security is mandatory; 'advised' = store MUST do this
findings.interpretation_b: 'Advised' = recommendation; store MAY ignore if they choose
findings.reasoning: PCI DSS compliance is NOT negotiable for payment systems, but requirements say 'advised' (wishy-washy). This creates legal/security risk. If store ignores advice and gets breached, is nopCommerce liable?
findings.recommendation: Use mandatory language: 'The system SHALL enforce HTTPS/TLS for all payment pages. Administrators MUST configure SSL certificate. Failure to enable SSL SHALL prevent payment method activation'

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) Admin Permissions
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements define admin capabilities (view orders, configure settings, manage products) but do NOT specify role-based access control allowing admins with limited permissions (e.g., warehouse staff view inventory only; marketing staff manage discounts only)
findings.where_expected: Admin features section or security sub-feature
findings.impact: All admins must have full access; operational risk of accidental/malicious data modification. Cannot enforce principle of least privilege. No separation of duties.
findings.reasoning: REQ002 mentions 'customer roles' (Registered vs Guest) but nothing about admin role granularity. Multi-store and multi-currency suggest enterprise use, which requires admin role segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Create feature: 'Admin Role-Based Access Control'. Support roles: SuperAdmin (full access), CatalogManager (products/categories/manufacturers), OrderManager (view/process orders), DiscountManager (coupons/gift cards), StoreManager (settings), WarehouseManager (inventory), CustomerService (view customer accounts, process refunds). Each role SHALL have explicit permission set definable by SuperAdmin. Audit all role-based access attempts.

findings.finding_title: Duplicate Currency Selection Requirements
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to select my preferred currency and see all prices converted, So that I understand costs in my local monetary system
findings.reasoning: REQ001 (F01_Currency Selection) and REQ024 (F24_Currency Selection) are functionally identical. Both describe the same capability: users selecting currencies from a dropdown, with automatic price recalculation and display in the selected currency using configured exchange rates. This duplication indicates either a requirements gathering error or intentional re-emphasis of an important feature.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate into a single requirement. If the feature is critical to the business case, clearly articulate why it appears twice (e.g., 'critical for international sales'). Update requirements numbering to eliminate the duplicate.

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Use of 'Currency Selection' Requirement
findings.current_usage: REQ001 and REQ024 both describe the same functionality (currency selection from dropdown menu) but are listed as separate requirements
findings.proposed_definition: A single unified requirement for currency selection should replace both REQ001 and REQ024
findings.reasoning: Requirements REQ001 (F01_Currency Selection) and REQ024 (F24_Currency Selection) contain nearly identical descriptions. This redundancy suggests either: (1) accidental duplication during requirements gathering, or (2) unclear business logic regarding whether currency selection appears in multiple locations. The requirements use identical wording: 'The system shall allow users to select their preferred currency from a dropdown menu...'
findings.recommendation: Consolidate REQ001 and REQ024 into a single requirement or clearly differentiate their functional scope. If they represent different UI locations (header vs. footer), explicitly state the distinction in the requirement description.

findings.finding_title: Authentication-Dependent Features Need Decomposition
findings.business_story: As a system architect, I want a clear decomposition of authentication features by granularity level, So that development teams can prioritize independently
findings.reasoning: REQ002 bundles multiple authentication capabilities into a single 'User Authentication' feature: (1) Registration with multiple methods, (2) Login with security features, (3) Account management with multiple sub-features (addresses, order history, downloadable products, etc.). This is too coarse for implementation planning and testing. Additionally, other requirements (REQ026, REQ052-057) re-specify login components without clear separation from core authentication.
findings.recommendation: Decompose F02 into granular features: F02a (Registration Flow), F02b (Login and Session Management), F02c (Account Profile Management), F02d (Security Enforcement). Each should have its own business story and acceptance criteria. Consolidate scattered login requirements (REQ052-057) under F02b.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Concurrent Cart Modifications by Multiple Devices
findings.scenario_text: Given a customer adds product A (quantity 2) to cart on Desktop, then opens the cart on Mobile device and adds product B (quantity 1), When returning to Desktop and clicking 'Update Cart', Then the system should reconcile both additions without losing either item or causing cart state conflicts; if Desktop session is stale, display 'Cart was modified on another device - please refresh'
findings.reasoning: Multi-device shopping scenarios are increasingly common. Cart synchronization is critical to prevent lost items, duplicate charges, or data inconsistencies. No scenario in requirements explicitly covers concurrent cart access from multiple devices/browsers.
findings.recommendation: Add edge case scenario testing cart consistency when accessed from multiple devices simultaneously. Document synchronization behavior (last-write-wins, merge, or conflict notification) in system design.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Coupon Code Usage Limits Per Customer
findings.scenario_text: Given a coupon code 'LIMITONE' is configured with 'Usage limit: 1 per customer', When Customer A applies the coupon successfully on their first order, and later attempts to apply the same coupon on a second order, Then the system should reject the coupon with message 'This coupon code has reached its usage limit for your account' and prevent application
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention usage limitations (REQ043) for coupon codes, but no scenario explicitly tests per-customer usage limits or the customer-facing validation error. This is critical for promotional enforcement and fraud prevention.
findings.recommendation: Add scenario testing per-customer coupon code usage limits, including error handling when limit is reached. Test combination of account-wide limits vs. global coupon usage limits.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Attribute-Based Price Adjustments in Cart
findings.scenario_text: Given product 'T-Shirt' has attribute 'Size' with adjustments: Small (+$0), Medium (+$0), Large (+$2.00), XL (+$5.00), When a customer adds Size Large T-Shirt @ $15.00 base price to cart showing $17.00 line total, then updates the attribute from Large to XL while in cart, Then the system should recalculate price to $20.00 (base $15 + $5 adjustment); if this affects tier pricing or free shipping threshold, those should also be recalculated
findings.reasoning: Requirements describe product attributes with price adjustments (REQ030, REQ029), but no comprehensive scenario tests how attribute changes in the cart trigger cascading recalculations (price, shipping eligibility, tier pricing, discount applicability).
findings.recommendation: Add edge case scenario testing attribute modification impacts on cart totals, shipping costs, and promotion eligibility. Test combinations of multiple attributes affecting price. Test attribute removal if applicable.

findings.finding_title: Ambiguous 'Configurable' Without Bounds
findings.text_excerpt: The number of autocomplete results displayed is configurable (e.g., show top 10 matches). The minimum search term length is configurable (e.g., minimum 3 characters).
findings.interpretation_a: Administrator can set ANY arbitrary number for autocomplete results (including 0, 1, or 1000000)
findings.interpretation_b: Administrator can configure within reasonable bounds (e.g., 3-50 results)
findings.reasoning: Without explicit min/max bounds, implementations might allow nonsensical values. If admin sets autocomplete to 10000 results, performance degrades. No guidance on what 'reasonable' is.
findings.recommendation: Specify: 'Autocomplete results range from minimum 1 to maximum 50 results, defaulting to 10'

findings.finding_title: Missing Error Handling: Inventory Overselling in Concurrent Orders
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements validate stock availability (REQ030, REQ040) but do NOT specify handling when two customers simultaneously order the last unit of a product (race condition leading to negative inventory)
findings.where_expected: REQ030/REQ040 Quantity Selection/Validation should include concurrency error handling
findings.impact: System may sell more units than in stock; supplier shortfall; customer dissatisfaction when order cannot be fulfilled; financial loss
findings.reasoning: Stock validation is checked against current inventory but no pessimistic locking or optimistic concurrency control specified. High-traffic stores experience race conditions regularly.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When multiple customers simultaneously checkout with the last N units of a product, the system SHALL: (1) Lock inventory row during checkout to prevent concurrent decrements, (2) Reserve stock on cart addition (not just at checkout), (3) If stock exhausted during checkout, display error to second customer with option to backorder or retry, (4) Send backorder confirmation email if customer chooses backorder, (5) Notify admin of backorder need'

findings.finding_title: Missing Performance NFR: Page Load Time and Scalability
findings.what_is_missing: No SLA for page load times, concurrent user capacity, or database query performance. No mention of caching, CDN, database optimization, or load testing thresholds.
findings.where_expected: Non-Functional Requirements or Performance section
findings.impact: System may be deployed on inadequate infrastructure; no baseline for performance regression; no SLA to defend against customer complaints about slowness
findings.reasoning: 150+ requirements are purely functional. Not a single requirement specifies 'Homepage shall load in < 2 seconds' or 'System shall handle 1000 concurrent users'. Critical for e-commerce success.
findings.recommendation: Add NFRs: 'Homepage and product pages SHALL load in < 2 seconds (p95). Search results SHALL return in < 1 second for queries matching < 1000 products. Checkout SHALL complete within 8 pages with < 5 second per-page response time. System SHALL support minimum 1000 concurrent users. Database queries SHALL execute in < 100ms (p95). Cache hit rate for static content SHALL exceed 95%'

findings.finding_title: Missing Cross-Cutting Concern: Rate Limiting and Abuse Prevention
findings.what_is_missing: No requirements for rate limiting on: (1) Login attempts (REQ052 mentions lockout but not rate limit details), (2) API endpoints, (3) Search queries, (4) Password reset emails, (5) Cart/order operations by bot/scraper
findings.where_expected: Security or Anti-Abuse cross-cutting concern
findings.impact: Brute force attacks on login; DDoS via search flooding; email abuse via mass password resets; inventory scraping; resource exhaustion
findings.reasoning: REQ056-057 mention brute-force protection vaguely but no rate limiting for other endpoints. Operational security critical for any production system.
findings.recommendation: Add NFR: 'System SHALL implement rate limiting for: (1) Login: max 5 failed attempts per email per 30 minutes, then 30-minute lockout. (2) Password reset email: max 3 requests per email per hour. (3) Search API: max 10 queries per IP per minute. (4) Cart operations: max 100 additions per customer per hour (detect bots). (5) All endpoints: max 1000 requests per IP per minute (configurable per endpoint). Rate limit violations SHALL log for admin review and trigger alerts if threshold exceeded.'

findings.finding_title: Ambiguous Definition of 'Continue Button' Across Multiple Steps
findings.current_usage: REQ065, REQ074, REQ081, REQ089 all reference 'Continue' buttons but with different contexts (billing → shipping, shipping → payment, payment → order confirmation)
findings.proposed_definition: A context-dependent action button present at each checkout step that validates the current step's data and advances the workflow to the next sequential step; button behavior and enabled state are determined by step-specific validation rules
findings.reasoning: The term 'Continue Button' appears in 4 separate requirements covering different checkout steps. While the functional intent is consistent, the requirement descriptions don't clearly define whether the button is always labeled 'Continue', whether behavior differs by step, or how validation failures affect button enablement. This ambiguity could lead to inconsistent implementation.
findings.recommendation: Create a unified 'Continue Button Behavior' glossary entry specifying: (1) button appears on all checkout steps except confirmation, (2) button is disabled until step-specific validation passes, (3) clicking continues to next step, (4) label remains 'Continue' across all steps (unless explicitly different, e.g., 'Confirm Order'). Reference this entry in all step-specific requirements.

findings.finding_title: Inconsistent Definition of 'Required Fields' Validation Across Forms
findings.current_usage: REQ062, REQ122, REQ139 all reference mandatory field enforcement and asterisk (*) marking, but requirements also state that 'All enabled fields... can be individually configured as required' (REQ062), creating ambiguity about which fields are always required vs. configurable
findings.proposed_definition: Form input fields marked with a red asterisk (*) indicator that must contain valid data before form submission; which fields are required is configurable by administrators but typically include critical fields (First name, Last name, Email, Address 1, City, Country, Postal code); the system enforces required field validation both client-side (JavaScript, immediate feedback) and server-side (security).
findings.reasoning: Multiple requirements mention mandatory field enforcement but don't clearly state: (1) which fields are required by default vs. configurable, (2) whether some fields are always required regardless of configuration, (3) what constitutes a 'critical field' vs. 'optional field'. This is especially problematic for address forms where requirements state both 'Default mandatory fields typically include...' (REQ062) and 'each field... can be individually enabled/disabled and set as required/optional' (REQ061), suggesting inconsistent requirements logic.
findings.recommendation: Define a standard set of 'always required' fields (e.g., First name, Last name, Email, Country, City, Address, Postal code) vs. 'conditionally required' fields (configurable by admin). Clarify that the asterisk mark indicates dynamic requirement status that may vary by configuration. Create a single 'Form Field Requirements Framework' entry to be referenced by all form-related requirements.

findings.finding_title: Undefined 'Checkout Attributes' Mechanism and Behavior
findings.current_usage: REQ042 mentions checkout attributes for gift wrapping with price adjustments and conditional display, but the glossary entry for 'Checkout Attributes' is overly generic and doesn't specify implementation mechanics
findings.proposed_definition: Dynamic, configurable form fields presented at specific points during checkout (shopping cart page, confirmed order page) that apply to the entire order or individual products. Checkout attributes support: control types (dropdown, radio buttons, checkboxes, color selector), optional price/weight adjustments per value, tax categories, conditional display rules (e.g., 'Shippable product required'), and required/optional designation.
findings.reasoning: REQ042 describes gift wrapping as a checkout attribute but introduces terms like 'control type', 'price adjustments', 'weight adjustments', 'tax category', and 'Shippable product required' without prior definition. The requirement doesn't explain: (1) when checkout attributes are collected relative to other checkout steps, (2) how multiple checkout attributes are ordered/displayed, (3) whether checkout attributes affect shipping cost calculations, (4) how they differ from product attributes.
findings.recommendation: Create a comprehensive glossary entry defining checkout attributes with: (1) collection point(s) in checkout flow (shopping cart page), (2) potential control types and how they're rendered, (3) price/weight adjustment calculation mechanics, (4) conditional display rule syntax/examples, (5) validation requirements. Clarify distinction between checkout attributes (order-level) and product attributes (product-level).

findings.finding_title: Ambiguous Definition of 'Responsive Design' and Device Support
findings.current_usage: REQ116 states 'automatically adapts to different screen sizes — desktop monitors, tablets, and mobile phones' but doesn't define what 'adapts' means (layout reflow, feature hiding, functionality preservation)
findings.proposed_definition: Responsive design is a web design approach where the user interface layout, content arrangement, and interactive elements dynamically adjust based on the viewport size and device type. Adaptations include: flexible grid layouts that reflow columns, CSS media queries for breakpoint-specific styling, relative sizing (percentages vs. fixed pixels), touch-friendly spacing for mobile interactions, and hamburger menus for mobile navigation. Core functionality and content remain accessible on all supported devices (desktop, tablet, mobile).
findings.reasoning: REQ116 describes responsive design symptoms but not mechanics. Stakeholders might interpret 'adapts' differently: Does hamburger menu replacement count as adaptation? Do features get hidden on mobile (reducing functionality)? Is the site mobile-first or desktop-first? The requirement lists 'Supported browsers: Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, and Apple Safari' but doesn't address mobile browser support (e.g., Chrome Mobile, Safari Mobile). The term 'responsive design' is used without defining minimum/maximum supported viewport sizes or breakpoints.
findings.recommendation: Expand the definition to specify: (1) Supported viewport ranges (e.g., 320px mobile to 2560px desktop), (2) Breakpoints and layout changes (e.g., 768px for tablet, 1024px for desktop), (3) Mobile-specific UI components (hamburger menu, touch-friendly buttons), (4) Content prioritization strategy (what's prioritized on mobile vs. desktop), (5) Supported device types and browsers including mobile variants, (6) Accessibility requirements across all viewport sizes. Reference this definition in other responsive design requirements.

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Guidance on 'Email Validation' and Account Activation
findings.current_usage: REQ051 states 'Email validation required (confirmation email sent)' and REQ054 states 'After clicking the reset link, the customer is directed to a page' but doesn't clarify whether registration is blocked until email is validated
findings.proposed_definition: Email validation registration method requires customers to verify their email address before account activation. Process: (1) Customer submits registration form with email address; (2) System sends a confirmation email to the provided address with a unique, time-limited activation link; (3) Customer clicks the link in the email to verify ownership; (4) Account is activated and customer can log in. During the verification period (before clicking the link), the account status is 'Pending - Email Validation' and login is blocked. The email validity period is configurable (e.g., 24-48 hours).
findings.reasoning: REQ051 mentions email validation as a registration method but doesn't clarify: (1) Account status during validation (can user log in immediately?), (2) What happens if the email is invalid (bounce handling), (3) Whether customers can resend the confirmation email, (4) What happens to unverified accounts after expiration (deleted, disabled, or resendable), (5) Interaction with 'Email validation required' vs. 'Admin approval required' (can both be enabled?). REQ054 (Forgot Password) references password reset links with configurable validity but doesn't clarify whether the same mechanism is used for registration email validation.
findings.recommendation: Define email validation registration as blocking login until verification. Specify: (1) Link validity period and extension mechanism (resend option), (2) Account status during pending verification, (3) Behavior for expired/invalid emails, (4) Error messages for each scenario, (5) Whether email validation and admin approval are independent or mutually exclusive. If both can be enabled, clarify the order of requirements (validation before approval, or approval before validation).

findings.finding_title: Search Functionality Fragments Across Multiple Requirements
findings.business_story: As a product manager, I want a cohesive search feature definition with clear separation of basic vs. advanced capabilities, So that stakeholders understand the complete search offering
findings.reasoning: Search is described across multiple scattered requirements: REQ005 (basic search with autocomplete), REQ014 (product search), REQ015 (advanced search), REQ016 (search results display), REQ018 (sorting), REQ019 (results per page). Each requirement adds incrementally to the feature without clear logical separation. The requirements don't explicitly distinguish between 'required for MVP' vs. 'nice-to-have' search capabilities.
findings.recommendation: Consolidate under F05 with clear sub-features: (1) Core Search (REQ005, REQ014), (2) Advanced Search (REQ015), (3) Results Management (REQ016, REQ018, REQ019). Each sub-feature should have acceptance criteria for what constitutes 'done'. This supports phased implementation (MVP with core, Phase 2 with advanced).

findings.finding_title: Product Display Information Is Fragmented Across Multiple Requirements
findings.business_story: As a product manager, I want a unified product information feature that clearly defines what content appears on product detail pages, So that the development team implements a consistent user experience
findings.reasoning: Product detail page content is scattered across REQ009 (Learn More button, product details), REQ028 (Product Details Display), REQ030 (Quantity Selection), REQ031 (Add to Wishlist), REQ032 (Compare), REQ033 (Email), REQ034 (Breadcrumbs), REQ035 (Reviews). Each requirement adds incrementally to the product page but there's no single unified feature definition showing the complete 'Product Details Page' composition. Designers need a holistic view of all elements that appear together on this page.
findings.recommendation: Create a single 'Product Details Page' feature that lists all page sections: (1) Product Identity (name, SKU, breadcrumb), (2) Media (images, videos), (3) Description (short & full), (4) Pricing & Availability, (5) Attributes & Variants, (6) Add to Cart Action, (7) Add to Wishlist, (8) Comparison, (9) Email, (10) Reviews & Ratings, (11) Related/Cross-sell. Each section should have a corresponding sub-feature with requirements. This enables UI/UX designers to create coherent wireframes.

findings.finding_title: Contact Form and Customer Support Features Need Consolidation
findings.business_story: As a support manager, I want a clear distinction between different customer support channels and their capabilities, So that we implement a cohesive support strategy across all touchpoints
findings.reasoning: Customer support capabilities are mentioned across REQ010 (documentation), REQ011 (forums), REQ013 (contact form), REQ133-140 (contact form details). The contact form details (REQ133-140) are extremely granular (name input field, email input field, enquiry input field, etc.) compared to higher-level forum/documentation requirements. This inconsistent granularity makes it hard to see the forest from the trees. Are name/email/enquiry input fields really separate 'features' or just implementation details of 'contact form'?
findings.recommendation: Restructure support features hierarchically: F09 (Customer Support) → F09a (Contact Form), F09b (Forum), F09c (Documentation). Only F09a should detail specific form fields (they're implementation, not features). Consolidate REQ133-140 into F09a with a single 'Contact Form' requirement rather than individual field requirements. This maintains appropriate abstraction levels across feature definitions.

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Inventory and Stock Management Visibility
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to see if a product is in stock, out of stock, or available for pre-order, So that I know whether I can purchase it immediately or if there will be a delay
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention 'stock availability' and 'inventory tracking' in the context of validation (REQ029: 'The system validates... stock availability') and 'availability status' display (REQ028: 'availability status'), but there is NO dedicated requirement specifying HOW stock information is displayed to customers.
