The requirements don't clarify: (1) Where is 'In Stock' / 'Out of Stock' text displayed, (2) Are pre-order and backorder statuses visible, (3) How is stock count displayed (exact number vs. 'Limited stock'), (4) Is there a 'back-in-stock notification' feature (REQ002 mentions it in account features)? This represents a gap in customer-facing inventory visibility.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F26: Stock Availability Display' with sub-features: (1) In-Stock Status Display, (2) Out-of-Stock Handling, (3) Pre-Order Indication, (4) Back-in-Stock Notification. Clearly specify where and how stock information appears on product pages, search results, and catalog listings. This enhances user experience and reduces support inquiries about availability.

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Product Availability Notifications
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to be notified when an out-of-stock product becomes available again, So that I can quickly purchase it before it sells out
findings.reasoning: REQ002 explicitly mentions 'back-in-stock subscriptions' as part of the 'My Account' page, and REQ003 mentions 'allow out-of-stock items' in wishlist configuration. This implies a feature exists for customers to subscribe to stock notifications, but there is NO dedicated requirement describing: (1) How customers subscribe to back-in-stock notifications, (2) Notification trigger conditions, (3) Notification delivery method (email), (4) Subscription management. The feature is implied but not explicitly defined.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F29: Product Availability Notifications' with sub-features: (1) Back-in-Stock Subscription (UI to subscribe), (2) Notification Triggering (when stock changes), (3) Notification Delivery (email), (4) Subscription Management (view/cancel subscriptions in My Account). This feature enables customers to stay engaged with desired out-of-stock products.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Cart Abandonment Notification
findings.outcome_description: The system will send a notification email when a customer abandons their shopping cart
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer adds items to cart but does not proceed to checkout within a configurable timeframe
findings.reasoning: REQ004 (Shopping Cart Management) defines cart features but does not specify any outcomes for abandoned carts. No recovery mechanism, reminder email, or notification is mentioned. This is a significant gap as cart abandonment is a major e-commerce concern with typical recovery rates improving conversion by 5-10%. Feature F04 (Shopping Cart Management) lacks this critical business outcome.
findings.recommendation: Define a specific requirement for cart abandonment recovery: timing (24-48 hours), notification mechanism (email), content (discount incentive options), and tracking of recovery effectiveness. Add to Feature F04 sub-features: 'Abandoned cart recovery email'

findings.finding_title: Null Outcome: Order Confirmation Email Not Explicitly Specified
findings.outcome_description: The system will send an order confirmation email to the customer after successful order placement
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer completes checkout and confirms order
findings.reasoning: While REQ099-REQ107 (Order Details/History) extensively define what information is displayed on order details pages, there is no explicit requirement stating that an order confirmation email SHALL be sent to the customer. The system displays order number, date, status after completion (REQ099) but the outcome of 'system sends confirmation email' is not articulated as a requirement. This is an implicit assumption, not an explicit outcome specification.
findings.recommendation: Create explicit requirement: 'The system shall send an order confirmation email containing order number, items, total, and delivery estimate to the customer immediately upon order completion, using the ConfigUrableOrderConfirmed message template.' Add to Feature F14 (Order Confirmation and Review) or create new F101_Order Confirmation Email sub-feature.

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Outcome: Guest Checkout Restrictions Undefined
findings.outcome_description: The system restrictions on guest checkout for downloadable products are not clearly specified in terms of outcomes
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer with downloadable products in cart attempts guest checkout
findings.reasoning: REQ050 states 'Guest checkout option is not available if all products in the cart are downloadable and Require registration for downloadable products is enabled.' REQ058 repeats this condition. However, the OUTCOME when this restriction is triggered is not specified: Does the system block checkout with error message? Does it force registration? Does it remove downloadable items? Does it auto-convert to registered user flow? The behavior is ambiguous. Also, REQ003 and F03 specify wishlists require registered users but the outcome when a guest tries to access wishlist is not specified.
findings.recommendation: Clarify the outcome: 'When a guest customer with downloadable products attempts checkout and the Require registration setting is enabled, the system SHALL: (1) display a notification message explaining the requirement, (2) block the guest checkout button, (3) offer registration or login as the only path forward.' Create specific requirement for this conditional behavior.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Stock Availability Verification at Order Completion
findings.outcome_description: The system will verify that all ordered products remain in stock immediately before order confirmation
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer reaches order confirmation step (REQ098) - time lag between adding to cart and confirming order
findings.reasoning: REQ098 (Order Verification) states 'stock verification' is performed on the Confirm Order step, but does not specify the outcome IF stock is no longer available. What happens if: a product sold out between cart and confirmation? Quantity reduced? Stock became unavailable for another customer? The requirement validates existence but not the outcome of validation failure. No specification of: error message, suggested actions, order blocking, stock holding mechanism, or customer notification.
findings.recommendation: Define outcome: 'If at order confirmation any item is found to be out of stock or insufficient quantity available, the system SHALL: (1) block order confirmation, (2) display which items are unavailable and remaining stock, (3) offer options: remove item, reduce quantity, or return to cart to modify.' Clarify whether stock is reserved during checkout or only validated at confirmation.

findings.finding_title: Passive Outcome: Contact Form Submission Handling
findings.outcome_description: The system will send an email notification to the support team and confirmation to the customer
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer submits contact form (F09, REQ013, REQ133-REQ140)
findings.reasoning: REQ139 states 'When a customer submits the Contact Us form, nopCommerce automatically sends an email notification to the store's designated email address' - this is passive voice describing system behavior. REQ138 mentions 'a confirmation message is displayed to the customer' but this is UI feedback, not a transactional outcome. There is no specification that the system ALSO sends a confirmation email to the customer (only a page message). No mention of: customer notification, receipt confirmation email, auto-reply mechanism, or ticket generation. The outcome is partially specified (admin notification) but missing customer communication outcome.
findings.recommendation: Clarify outcomes: (1) 'The system SHALL send a confirmation message to the customer's email address confirming receipt of their inquiry and providing a reference number.' (2) 'The system SHALL queue the support team notification email for dispatch via the message queue.' Define both directions of communication, expected timeframes, and any auto-reply mechanism.

findings.finding_title: Hanging Outcome: Refund Processing and Customer Notification
findings.outcome_description: The system will notify the customer when a refund is processed and payment status changes to 'Refunded'
findings.triggering_scenario: Administrator processes a refund from the admin panel
findings.reasoning: REQ101 defines Payment Status display including 'Partially Refunded' and 'Refunded' states, but there is NO requirement specifying the outcome when a refund is initiated or processed by the admin. The order details page displays the status (REQ101, REQ107) but how does the customer learn about the refund? Is there a notification email? What triggers the status change? When is the refund actually returned to the payment method? The refund action itself is not defined as a requirement outcome - it's assumed to exist via admin panel but not specified in customer-facing or business logic terms.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When an administrator processes a full or partial refund through the admin panel, the system SHALL: (1) update the order's Payment Status to Refunded or Partially Refunded, (2) send a refund notification email to the customer with refund amount, method, and expected return timeframe, (3) record the refund transaction for audit, (4) display refund status in the customer's order details.' Define refund workflows (full vs. partial) and automation conditions.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Shipping Status Update Notifications
findings.outcome_description: The system will send email notifications to the customer when shipping status changes
findings.triggering_scenario: Admin updates shipping status (Not Shipped → Shipped → Delivered) or shipment tracking is generated
findings.reasoning: REQ102 and REQ107 describe Shipping Status display (Not yet shipped, Partially shipped, Shipped, Delivered) but do not specify the outcome when status changes. The requirements state 'Tracking numbers are provided when shipments are created' but NO requirement specifies that the customer is NOTIFIED of shipment, receives tracking number, or gets status change notifications. Feature F18 (Order Management) describes status display but not the communication outcome when status transitions.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When a shipment is created, the system SHALL send a shipment notification email to the customer containing: tracking number, carrier information, estimated delivery date, and tracking URL. When shipping status is updated, the system SHALL send status update notifications (Order Shipped, Out for Delivery, Delivered).' Define each notification trigger and content requirements.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Minimum Order Amount Enforcement
findings.scenario_text: Given minimum order subtotal is configured to $50 in Order settings, When a customer attempts checkout with only $35 in cart, Then at the Confirm Order step, the system should display validation error 'Order subtotal is below the minimum required amount ($50)' and disable the 'Confirm Order' button until cart value reaches minimum
findings.reasoning: REQ093 and REQ098 mention minimum order amount validation (REQ093 states 'Minimum order subtotal and total amounts can be enforced'), but no explicit scenario tests the customer-facing behavior when minimum is not met during order confirmation.
findings.recommendation: Add scenario for minimum order validation at order confirmation step. Test both subtotal and grand total minimums, including behavior when discounts/coupons bring cart below minimum after application.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Tier Pricing and Quantity-Based Discounts
findings.scenario_text: Given product 'Bulk USB Cable' has tier pricing configured: 1-5 units @ $2.00, 6-10 units @ $1.80, 11+ units @ $1.50 per unit, When a customer adds 8 units to cart, Then unit price should automatically adjust to $1.80, line total recalculates to $14.40, and order totals update; if customer later increases quantity to 12, then price drops to $1.50 ($18.00 total)
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention tier pricing in REQ093 (order total calculation includes tier pricing), but no scenario explicitly demonstrates quantity-based pricing tiers and real-time price recalculation as quantities change in the cart.
findings.recommendation: Add data-driven scenario testing tier pricing at different quantity thresholds. Test price updates both when initially adding to cart and when modifying quantities in cart. Test boundary values (e.g., quantity exactly at tier breakpoint).

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Abandoned Cart Recovery and Persistence
findings.scenario_text: Given a guest customer adds 3 products to cart but does not proceed to checkout, When the same customer returns to the store 24 hours later via the same browser (session expired), Then the cart should be restored from persistent storage; if cookie-based cart was cleared, the system should retrieve cart from database using IP/device fingerprinting or email recovery mechanism
findings.reasoning: No requirement explicitly addresses cart persistence for guest users or abandoned cart recovery. This is a critical e-commerce feature for conversion recovery and customer experience.
findings.recommendation: Add scenario testing cart recovery for both registered and guest users across session boundaries. Document whether guest carts are persisted beyond session timeout, and if so, for how long. Test cart state when accessed from different browsers/devices.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for State/Province-Dependent Shipping Method Availability
findings.scenario_text: Given shipping method 'Next Day Air' is restricted to continental US (excludes AK, HI), When a customer enters shipping address in Alaska (state AK), Then on the Shipping Method step, 'Next Day Air' should be hidden/disabled from the available options with message 'This shipping method is not available for your location'; only 'Ground' and '2-Day Air' should display as available options
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention shipping method availability based on destination (REQ070 states 'available shipping options based on customer's shipping address'), but no scenario explicitly tests geographic restrictions or conditional availability based on state/region.
findings.recommendation: Add edge case scenarios testing shipping method availability based on geographic restrictions. Test boundary cases (state/country combinations at restriction limits). Test user feedback when preferred method becomes unavailable due to location change.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Real-Time Carrier Rate Calculation Timeout/Failure
findings.scenario_text: Given shipping is configured to use real-time UPS rates (via UPS API), When a customer enters shipping address during checkout but the UPS rate API is temporarily unavailable (timeout > 5 seconds), Then the system should: (1) display fallback message 'Shipping rates currently unavailable; using estimate' with previously cached rates, OR (2) allow customer to proceed with minimum shipping charge pending rate confirmation, OR (3) block checkout with error 'Unable to calculate shipping; please try again' - the requirement should specify which behavior
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention real-time carrier rate calculation support (REQ070), but no scenario addresses API failure, timeout handling, or fallback behavior when external rate service is unavailable. This is critical for checkout reliability.
findings.recommendation: Add exception scenarios testing real-time shipping rate failures: API timeout, invalid address format, service unavailability, incomplete response. Document system behavior (fallback, retry, block) and customer-facing messaging.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Product Attribute Combination Stock Tracking
findings.scenario_text: Given product 'Gaming Laptop' has attributes Color (Black, Silver) and Storage (256GB, 512GB) creating 4 combinations, with stock levels: Black+256GB (10), Black+512GB (0), Silver+256GB (5), Silver+512GB (12), When a customer selects attributes on product details page, Then the system should: (1) dynamically disable unavailable combinations (e.g., 'Black + 512GB' option grayed out), (2) show availability status based on selected combination ('Out of Stock' for Black+512GB, 'In Stock' for others), (3) prevent 'Add to Cart' when selected combination is out of stock
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention attribute combinations (REQ029, REQ030) but no scenario explicitly tests stock management per attribute combination variant, which is essential for products with multiple variants.
findings.recommendation: Add data-driven scenario testing stock levels per attribute combination. Test dynamic UI updates (disable options) when combinations are out of stock. Test stock depletion and restock scenarios for specific combinations.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Regional Tax Calculation Accuracy
findings.scenario_text: Given tax rules configured: (1) US CA (California) - 7.25%, (2) US TX (Texas) - 6.25%, (3) EU countries - VAT 19-23% with reverse charge rules for B2B, When a B2C customer from California orders $100 product, Then tax = $7.25 (Grand Total $107.25); When a B2B customer from same state orders, Then tax = $0 with 'Tax Exempt' status; When EU B2C customer orders, Then tax = 19% ($119) with VAT shown on invoice
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention tax calculation (REQ001, REQ047, REQ093) based on product tax categories and customer address, but no scenario tests complex regional tax rules, VAT application, tax exemptions for B2B, or reverse charge mechanisms critical for international compliance.
findings.recommendation: Add data-driven scenarios testing tax calculations across multiple regions/countries, B2B vs B2C status, VAT rules, tax-exempt scenarios, and tiered tax rates. Test that tax is recalculated if customer changes billing/shipping address during checkout.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Multi-Currency Exchange Rate Application
findings.scenario_text: Given exchange rates configured: 1 USD = 0.95 EUR, 1 USD = 109.5 JPY; store base currency is USD; product costs $100 USD, When a customer selects EUR currency and views product, Then display price should be 95 EUR (100 × 0.95); When adding to cart and viewing order summary, All amounts (product price, shipping, tax, total) should be consistently converted using the same exchange rate; if exchange rates update mid-checkout, document whether prices are locked or recalculated
findings.reasoning: REQ001 and REQ024 mention multi-currency support with automatic conversion, but no scenario tests exchange rate application consistency, rounding behavior, or handling of rate updates during checkout. This is critical for international transactions.
findings.recommendation: Add data-driven scenarios testing currency conversion accuracy across all checkout steps. Test exchange rate rounding (e.g., 95.5 EUR rounds to 95.50 or 96.00?). Test rate staleness: if rates update mid-checkout, are prices locked or refreshed? Test rare currency edge cases.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Password Reset Link Expiration
findings.scenario_text: Given password recovery link validity is configured to 7 days in Customer settings, When a customer requests password reset and receives recovery email, and waits 8 days to click the reset link, Then the system should display error 'Your password recovery link has expired. Please request a new recovery email' and redirect to forgot password page; customer cannot reset password with expired link
findings.reasoning: REQ054 mentions configurable password reset link validity, but no scenario tests the expiration enforcement or error handling when link is stale. This is important for account security.
findings.recommendation: Add exception scenario testing password reset link expiration. Test both within validity period (success) and after expiration (failure). Test that multiple reset requests generate new links. Test that user cannot reuse expired links.

findings.finding_title: Hanging Outcome: Discount Recalculation When Cart Items Become Invalid
findings.scenario: Customer applies a coupon 'BULK10' requiring minimum order of $500 with 2+ items in cart ($600 subtotal). Customer then removes items from cart, dropping subtotal to $350 with 1 item remaining, invalidating the coupon condition.
findings.predicted_outcome: System automatically detects coupon is no longer valid, removes the discount, recalculates order total, and displays notification 'Coupon no longer applies due to cart changes'
findings.realistic_alternative: System preserves the discount (discount amount frozen at 10% of original $600 = $60 applied to $350 subtotal for final total of $290), allowing customer to exploit the coupon
findings.absurd_alternative: System crashes when discount validation fails and requires customer to clear cart and restart
findings.reasoning: REQ043 defines coupon features (minimum order amount, usage limits, cumulative discounts) and REQ040 specifies cart quantity updates trigger recalculation, but the requirements do NOT specify: (1) whether discount validity is re-evaluated when cart changes, (2) which trigger re-evaluation (quantity change? item removal? quantity drop below minimum?), (3) the user-facing behavior when a discount becomes invalid (notification? silent removal? warning before removal?), (4) whether the customer is given option to add items back to re-apply the discount. The timing of validation is ambiguous.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit requirement: 'When cart contents change (quantity update, item removal), the system SHALL re-validate all applied coupon codes against current cart state. If a coupon no longer qualifies (subtotal below minimum, item category removed, quantity requirement unmet), the system SHALL: (A) remove the discount, (B) recalculate order total, (C) display a notification message explaining why the coupon was removed, with a re-apply button if conditions can be re-met by adding items.' Define the re-validation trigger point and notification content.

findings.finding_title: Missing Default: Behavior When All Shipping Methods Are Disabled or Unavailable
findings.scenario: Customer reaches the Shipping Method step (REQ070) but no shipping methods are available for their address. Scenario: store has configured only 'Ground Shipping - USA Only' and customer's address is in Canada.
findings.predicted_outcome: System displays error message 'No shipping methods available for your address. Please update your shipping address or contact support.' and prevents checkout from advancing
findings.realistic_alternative: System auto-falls back to 'Pickup in Store' method, assuming customer will retrieve order locally without explicit notification
findings.absurd_alternative: System defaults to 'Teleportation - Instant Delivery ($0.00)' as a fallback shipping method
findings.reasoning: REQ070-REQ076 extensively define the shipping method selection flow with pre-selected defaults and validation, but are completely SILENT on: (1) what happens when NO shipping methods are available, (2) whether an error state is shown or if a phantom 'default' method appears, (3) whether checkout is blocked or proceeds, (4) what error message (if any) is displayed to the customer, (5) whether the system suggests address correction or provides contact info. The requirements assume at least one shipping method will always be available, which is not guaranteed in a misconfigured system.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'If no shipping methods are available for the customer's selected shipping address, the system SHALL: (1) block advancement from the Shipping Method step, (2) display a clear error message: "We are unable to ship to your selected address. Available shipping destinations include: [list]. Please update your address or contact our support team at [email/phone].", (3) provide an easy path back to the shipping address step to modify the address, (4) enable manual admin override for special cases.' Define what constitutes 'no available methods' and recovery options.

findings.finding_title: Weasel Word Inconsistency: 'Can' vs 'Shall'
findings.text_excerpt: REQ001: 'The system shall allow users to select their preferred currency' vs REQ007: 'nopCommerce supports homepage promotional content through configurable widgets'. The cart 'can either display the cart page immediately or keep the user on the current page'.
findings.interpretation_a: 'Shall' = mandatory requirement; 'Can' = optional/capability that may not be implemented
findings.interpretation_b: 'Shall' and 'can' both describe functionality that exists but level of implementation commitment unclear
findings.reasoning: RFC 2119 keywords (MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY, CAN) are not used consistently. Some reqs use 'shall', others use 'can'. REQ017 says 'can either display...or keep on page'—two mutually exclusive options without specifying which is DEFAULT. This creates implementation ambiguity.
findings.recommendation: Use RFC 2119 keywords exclusively: 'SHALL' (mandatory), 'SHOULD' (recommended), 'MAY' (optional). Example: 'The system SHALL display the cart page immediately after adding a product (configurable)' or 'The system MAY optionally display...'

findings.finding_title: Undefined Reference: 'The System' - Core vs Theme
findings.text_excerpt: REQ007: 'The system shall display promotional banners on the homepage'. REQ008: 'The homepage displays product boxes containing product image'. REQ026: 'The header of the public store displays authentication links'.
findings.interpretation_a: 'The system' = nopCommerce core platform (database, backend logic)
findings.reasoning: Requirements don't distinguish between nopCommerce core functionality vs theme-specific behavior. Banners are theme-dependent; they're not part of core nopCommerce. Who is responsible for homepage layout? Are these core requirements or theme customization?
findings.recommendation: Distinguish: 'nopCommerce core system SHALL provide' vs 'The theme/storefront SHALL display' vs 'Administrators can configure via admin panel to display'

findings.finding_title: Ambiguous Language: 'Support' - Passive Capability vs Requirement
findings.text_excerpt: REQ001: 'nopCommerce supports multi-currency configuration'. REQ011: 'nopCommerce includes a built-in forum system'. REQ006: 'support hierarchical parent-child relationships'.
findings.interpretation_a: 'Supports' = feature exists in codebase and can be used
findings.reasoning: 'Supports' is passive and non-committal. Does it mean the feature is enabled by default? Can it be disabled? Must it be used? REQ001 says 'supports multi-currency' but doesn't say if it's required or if single-currency stores are acceptable.
findings.recommendation: Use precise language: 'The system SHALL provide' (mandatory), 'The system MAY provide' (optional), 'Administrators CAN configure' (admin control), or 'The system is compatible with' (plugin-based features)

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario: Email Template Customization and Personalization
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements mention email sending (order confirmation, password reset, welcome email) but do NOT specify: (1) Whether emails are templated or hard-coded, (2) How admins customize templates, (3) Personalization tokens (e.g., %CUSTOMER_NAME%, %ORDER_NUMBER%), (4) Multi-language email variants
findings.where_expected: Email/Notification feature or admin configuration section
findings.impact: All customers receive identical generic emails; no personalization reduces engagement and conversion; admins cannot customize messaging for brand voice; non-English customers receive English-only emails
findings.reasoning: REQ002, REQ054-055, REQ139 describe email sending but as black boxes. No mention of template management, variable substitution, or localization.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'The system SHALL support configurable email templates accessible via Content Management. Each template SHALL support personalization tokens: %CUSTOMER_NAME%, %ORDER_NUMBER%, %ORDER_TOTAL%, %SHIPPING_ADDRESS%, %PRODUCT_LIST%. Admins SHALL be able to customize template subject, body, and footer per template type (Welcome, Order Confirmation, Refund, Password Reset). System SHALL support multi-language variants of each template. Emails SHALL render correctly in major email clients (Outlook, Gmail, Apple Mail)'

findings.finding_title: Missing Completeness: Internationalization (i18n) Scope
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements mention multi-currency and multi-language but do NOT specify: (1) Which UI elements are translatable, (2) How admins manage translations, (3) RTL language support (Arabic, Hebrew), (4) Date/time formatting per locale, (5) Translation workflow (crowdsourcing, professional translation?)
findings.where_expected: Internationalization (i18n) or Localization (l10n) feature or cross-cutting NFR
findings.impact: UI may display half-translated; translations missing for new features; right-to-left languages broken; dates formatted incorrectly for locales (DD/MM/YYYY vs MM/DD/YYYY confusion)
findings.reasoning: REQ024 mentions currency, REQ119 mentions language, but no comprehensive i18n specification. nopCommerce is internationally distributed; i18n is critical.
findings.recommendation: Add feature: 'Internationalization and Localization (i18n/l10n)'. Requirements: (1) All customer-facing UI text MUST be translatable (not hard-coded). (2) Admin interface to manage translations per language. (3) Support right-to-left layout for RTL languages (automatic layout flip, text direction). (4) Locale-aware formatting: dates (use locale date format), numbers (thousand separators, decimal point), currency display. (5) Fallback language: if translation missing, display in default language, not English. (6) Mark missing translations in admin for translator review.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Product Availability Notifications (Stock Alerts)
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements validate stock and prevent overselling but do NOT specify: (1) Allowing customers to subscribe for 'back in stock' notifications, (2) Admin notification when stock threshold reached, (3) Automatic email when product is restocked
findings.where_expected: Product Catalog or Notification features section
findings.impact: Customers cannot request notifications; lost sales when product is restocked (customer never knows); admin manually monitors inventory with no alerts
findings.reasoning: REQ002 vaguely mentions 'back-in-stock subscriptions' but no detail. REQ030/REQ040 validate stock but nothing about notifications. Critical for e-commerce engagement.
findings.recommendation: Create feature: 'Product Availability Notifications'. Must include: (1) Out-of-stock product detail page shows 'Notify me when in stock' button. (2) Customer enters email, subscribes to restocking alert. (3) When admin restocks product (inventory > 0), system sends email to all subscribed customers. (4) Admin dashboard shows 'Products with pending notifications' count. (5) Customers can manage subscriptions in My Account. (6) Auto-unsubscribe after notification sent (or configurable to keep subscription).'

findings.finding_title: Missing Accessibility (a11y) Requirements
findings.what_is_missing: No requirements for WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance: (1) Alt text on product images, (2) Keyboard navigation (no mouse required), (3) Color contrast ratios for text, (4) ARIA labels for screen readers, (5) Form field labeling for accessibility
findings.where_expected: Non-Functional Requirements or Accessibility section
findings.impact: Visually impaired users cannot purchase; keyboard-only users blocked; ADA/AODA compliance violations; market segment unreachable
findings.reasoning: Entirely absent from 153 requirements. Regulatory requirement in US (ADA), Canada (AODA), EU (DIRECTIVE 2016/2102). No e-commerce platform can ignore this.
findings.recommendation: Add NFR: 'The system SHALL comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards: (1) All product images, banners, icons SHALL have descriptive alt text. (2) All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) SHALL be keyboard navigable. (3) Text colors SHALL have contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 (for normal text) or >= 3:1 (for large text). (4) Form inputs SHALL have associated labels. (5) Focus indicators SHALL be visible. (6) Screen reader compatibility via semantic HTML and ARIA landmarks. (7) Error messages SHALL be associated with input fields. Audit accessibility quarterly with automated tools and manual testing.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Customer Communication for Out-of-Stock Products in Cart
findings.what_is_missing: When a customer has items in cart and those items become out-of-stock, no requirement specifies: (1) System notification to customer, (2) Email about unavailability, (3) Option to remove from cart or request backorder, (4) Checkout blocking behavior
findings.where_expected: Shopping Cart or Inventory features
findings.impact: Customer arrives at cart, items disappear silently, confusion and frustration. Sales lost. No customer communication.
findings.reasoning: REQ040 validates stock at quantity update but not at checkout start. If item goes OOS between cart addition and checkout (flash sale, other customer's simultaneous order), no specified outcome.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When a customer accesses their cart and an item is no longer in stock: (1) Mark item with 'Out of Stock' badge on cart page, (2) Disable quantity edit and 'Add to Cart' actions for that item, (3) Display message 'This item is temporarily unavailable' below product name, (4) Offer 'Remove from cart' and 'Notify me when back in stock' options, (5) Send email to customer within 1 hour notifying of availability change, (6) Allow checkout to proceed only after removing OOS items (warn customer before removing).'

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Order Tracking with Customer Visibility
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements define order statuses (REQ099-107) but do NOT specify: (1) Customer-visible order tracking (where is my package?), (2) Shipment tracking URL/number display, (3) Estimated delivery date, (4) Proactive customer notifications on status changes, (5) Carrier integration for real-time tracking
findings.where_expected: Order Display or Customer Notification features
findings.impact: Customers cannot track orders; support load increases from 'where is my order' inquiries; no proactive status updates. Poor customer experience.
findings.reasoning: REQ102 mentions shipping status but customer doesn't see it. Admin views it but customer can't check order from My Account. Critical for customer satisfaction.
findings.recommendation: Create feature: 'Customer Order Tracking'. Requirements: (1) Order details page shows current status with progress bar (Order Received → Processing → Shipped → Delivered). (2) Display tracking number as clickable link to carrier tracking page. (3) Show estimated delivery date (calculated from shipping method + order date). (4) Automatically send email to customer when order ships (include tracking number). (5) Send proactive notifications on status changes (order processed, shipped, out for delivery, delivered). (6) Optional carrier API integration: Real-time tracking syncs with carrier (e.g., FedEx/UPS API).'

findings.finding_title: Missing Configuration Completeness: Admin Settings for Business Rules
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements mention configurable settings throughout but do NOT specify: (1) Central admin interface to view/modify all configurable parameters, (2) Settings categorization and organization, (3) Settings validation (e.g., min/max values), (4) Settings export/import for backup or multi-store sync, (5) Settings audit log (who changed what, when)
findings.where_expected: Admin Configuration feature or System Administration section
findings.impact: Admins confused by scattered settings; no single source of truth; settings changed accidentally without tracking; multi-store configuration error-prone
findings.reasoning: 150+ requirements mention configurable elements ('configurable X' appears 40+ times) but no unified settings management architecture. Flo noted this gap.
findings.recommendation: Add feature: 'System Configuration Management'. Must include: (1) Central admin settings dashboard with tabs (Catalog, Checkout, Payments, Shipping, Email, Security). (2) Settings input with validation: text, dropdown, numeric (with min/max), toggle, color picker, time/date. (3) Settings descriptions and help text. (4) Save/Cancel buttons with confirmation. (5) Settings export to JSON for backup/multi-store sync. (6) Settings audit log: timestamp, admin user, setting name, old value, new value. (7) Rollback capability: restore settings from previous snapshot.'

findings.finding_title: Undefined Business Logic for 'View Mode' Display Options
findings.current_usage: REQ025 mentions both Grid and List view modes for product listings, but doesn't define how the view mode is triggered, persisted, or displayed
findings.proposed_definition: A user-selectable presentation format for product listings where Grid view displays products in a multi-column card layout, and List view displays products in a single-column layout with more detailed information; the selected mode persists within the current session.
findings.reasoning: REQ025 states 'The system shall provide options to switch between grid and list views for search results' but doesn't define: (1) how users trigger the switch (dropdown, icon buttons, toggle), (2) which format is the default, (3) whether persistence is session-wide or page-specific, (4) what content differences exist between views. REQ006 references 'configurable view modes (grid/list)' but again lacks specifics.
findings.recommendation: Expand the definition to specify: (1) UI control type for switching (icon buttons are mentioned in REQ025 as 'grid icon / list icon'), (2) default view mode per page type, (3) persistence scope (session-wide across category pages), (4) content differences (Grid: card layout; List: single column with descriptions), (5) whether view mode selection is global or per-page.

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Definition of 'Add to Cart' Behavior from Product Variants
findings.current_usage: REQ017 states 'For products with required attributes... the user is redirected to the product details page' while REQ029 states 'Before adding, the user can select product attributes' on the product details page itself
findings.proposed_definition: The 'Add to cart' action's behavior depends on product configuration: (1) Simple products or products without required attributes: add directly with default quantity 1; (2) Products with required attributes on listing pages: redirect to product details page for attribute selection; (3) Products with optional attributes on listing pages: add with default quantity 1, optionally redirecting for customization; (4) On product details page: allow attribute selection before adding.
findings.reasoning: REQ017 clearly states redirection for required attributes, but the definition doesn't account for optional vs. required attributes distinction. REQ029 assumes the user is already on the product details page. This creates ambiguity: What happens when a user clicks 'Add to cart' on a listing page for a product with optional (not required) attributes? Are they redirected, or added with defaults?
findings.recommendation: Revise the definition to explicitly address: (1) Required attribute scenario: always redirect to product details page on listing page 'Add to cart' clicks, (2) Optional attribute scenario: add with defaults on listing pages, optionally offering a redirect link, (3) Product details page scenario: allow full attribute selection before adding. Clarify distinction between required vs. optional attributes in the Product Attributes glossary entry.

findings.finding_title: Missing Definition of 'Back Button' Scope and Data Preservation
findings.current_usage: REQ073, REQ080, REQ088, REQ094 all reference Back buttons enabling backward navigation in checkout, but don't specify whether all previously entered data is preserved across multiple back-forth cycles
findings.proposed_definition: A navigation button present on each checkout step (except the first step: Billing Address) that returns the customer to the previous step while preserving all previously entered data (addresses, shipping selection, payment method) within the current session. Data persists through multiple back-forth navigation cycles until checkout completion or session termination.
findings.reasoning: While requirements state 'preserving all previously entered data', they don't clarify: (1) data scope (all form fields, selected options, etc.), (2) preservation duration (session only, or persistent across sessions), (3) behavior if data validation rules change between back-forth navigation, (4) handling of address deletions/modifications made after back navigation. REQ073 says 'while preserving all previously entered data' but REQ088 says 'preserving entered credit card data in the current session', introducing session-specific language.
findings.recommendation: Standardize the Back button definition across all checkout step requirements to consistently state: (1) Button appears on steps 2+ only, (2) Clicking Back navigates to the previous step, (3) All step-specific data is preserved in session memory, (4) Data persists through unlimited back-forth navigation, (5) Data is cleared only upon successful order completion or explicit session termination/timeout. Avoid session-specific language in some requirements but not others.

findings.finding_title: Undefined Scope of 'Order Confirmation Page' Content and Behavior
findings.current_usage: REQ092 references 'Confirm Order step' (checkout workflow), REQ099 references 'Order Completed page', and REQ100 references 'Order Details page', creating ambiguity about whether these are the same page or different pages
findings.proposed_definition: Multiple order-related pages exist in distinct contexts: (1) Confirm Order step: Final checkout step before order placement, displays complete order summary with all details and 'Confirm' button; (2) Order Completed page: Redirect page shown immediately after successful order placement, displays order summary, number, date, total; (3) Order Details page: Accessed via My Account → Orders list, displays comprehensive order information with additional actions (print, PDF, reorder), accessible anytime after checkout.
findings.reasoning: The three terms reference overlapping functionality but don't clarify relationships. REQ099 states 'After successful order placement, the Order Completed page (or Order Details page)' using 'or', suggesting they might be the same page with a 'Disable Order completed page' option redirecting to Order Details. However, subsequent requirements treat Order Details page as a separate, persistent page. Users unfamiliar with nopCommerce architecture might not understand: (1) When each page is shown, (2) What content differs between pages, (3) Whether the Order Completed page is a temporary redirect or persistent page.
findings.recommendation: Create three distinct glossary entries: (1) 'Confirm Order Step': Last checkout step before order submission, (2) 'Order Completed Page': Post-checkout confirmation page (potentially disabled via configuration), (3) 'Order Details Page': Persistent page accessible via customer account. Clarify in requirements the 'Disable Order completed page' configuration's effect (redirects directly to Order Details page). Define what content is displayed on each page and how they interrelate.

findings.finding_title: Missing Definition of 'Terms of Service Agreement' Compliance Flow
findings.current_usage: REQ049 states the requirement can appear on 'shopping cart page' or 'confirm order page' but doesn't clarify the enforcement logic when both options are configured
findings.proposed_definition: A mandatory checkbox agreement requiring customers to accept the store's Terms of Service before proceeding with purchase. The agreement can be enforced at one of two checkout points: (1) Shopping Cart Page: Checkbox appears on the shopping cart; checkout button is disabled until checked; (2) Confirm Order Page: Checkbox appears on the final order confirmation step before 'Confirm' button. Only one enforcement point is active per configuration. The Terms of Service content is managed as a Topic with system name 'ConditionsOfUse' and displayed in a popup/modal when the checkbox link is clicked.
findings.reasoning: REQ049 states 'Two options are available' suggesting mutual exclusivity, but doesn't explicitly state that only one can be active. The requirement doesn't clarify: (1) What happens if both are enabled (conflict resolution), (2) Whether the requirement can appear in multiple locations with different content, (3) Whether users must agree at both steps if somehow both are enabled, (4) How the Terms of Service content is versioned if updated mid-customer-journey.
findings.recommendation: Clarify that Terms of Service agreement enforcement is mutually exclusive: only one location (shopping cart or confirm order) can be active. Define default configuration. Specify that the Topic content ('ConditionsOfUse') is version-controlled and customers must agree to the version displayed at the time of checkout. If future requirements need agreement at multiple steps, revise this definition accordingly.

findings.finding_title: Undefined Behavior of 'Estimate Shipping' Across Multiple Checkout Points
findings.current_usage: REQ037 mentions shipping estimation on product details page, REQ046 mentions it on shopping cart page, but requirements don't clarify whether estimates are available before/after address entry or how they affect the checkout flow
findings.proposed_definition: Estimate Shipping is a feature allowing customers to view available shipping methods and costs without committing to an address or entering full checkout. Available at two points: (1) Product Details Page: 'Estimate shipping' popup allows entering country/state/zip to see shipping options for that specific product; (2) Shopping Cart Page: 'Estimate Shipping' button opens popup for current cart contents, allowing shipping preview. Both popups display available methods, descriptions, and costs. Estimates update order totals but don't lock in the shipping selection until the shipping method step in checkout. Address information entered in estimate popups may pre-populate checkout address fields.
findings.reasoning: Requirements state shipping estimation is available on both product details and cart pages but don't clarify: (1) Whether addresses entered in estimates are remembered, (2) How estimates interact with saved customer addresses, (3) Whether estimates affect cart contents (can they view shipping before fully populating the cart?), (4) Whether estimated costs are guaranteed or can change during checkout if address changes, (5) Whether the estimate addresses must be provided or if default store address is used.
findings.recommendation: Create a unified 'Shipping Estimation Framework' glossary entry covering: (1) Availability points (product page, cart page, both), (2) Data entry requirements (country/state/zip at minimum, no full address required), (3) Output information (methods, costs, delivery times), (4) Persistence of estimate data (session-only), (5) Pre-population of checkout fields using estimate address, (6) Cost guarantees (estimates may change if address changes in checkout). Reference this entry in product details and cart-related requirements.

findings.finding_title: Conflicting Role-Based Access Control Terminology
findings.current_usage: Requirements refer to 'customer role' (REQ006, REQ051, REQ070) and 'customer group' (REQ043) interchangeably without clear definition of whether these are synonymous or different concepts
findings.proposed_definition: Customer Role (also referred to as Customer Group in some contexts) is a classification assigned to a customer that determines access permissions and business rule application. Roles include: (1) Guests/Anonymous: Unregistered users viewing the storefront; (2) Registered: Users with accounts, can access wishlists, order history, reward points; (3) Administrators: Store staff with full system access. Roles control visibility of products/categories (ACL restrictions), discount eligibility, forum permissions, and feature availability.
findings.reasoning: Requirements use 'customer role' and 'customer group' inconsistently. REQ051 states 'After registration, the customer is assigned to the 'Registered' customer role' while REQ043 references discount features 'assigned to a customer role'. The terms appear synonymous in usage, but nopCommerce documentation sometimes distinguishes 'roles' (permission-based) from 'groups' (discount/business rule groupings). This inconsistency could confuse developers implementing ACL or discount logic.
findings.recommendation: Standardize terminology: choose either 'Customer Role' or 'Customer Group' and use consistently throughout all requirements. If they represent different concepts in the actual system, provide explicit definitions distinguishing their purposes and technical implementation. If synonymous, create a single glossary entry and enforce consistent terminology across all requirements.

findings.finding_title: Shopping Cart Feature Has Multiple Interconnected Capabilities
findings.business_story: As a development lead, I want each cart capability separated into independent features with clear implementation boundaries, So that team members can work on different cart features in parallel without blocking each other
findings.reasoning: F04 (Shopping Cart) bundles many independent capabilities: add to cart, view cart, update quantities, remove items, apply coupons, apply gift cards, gift wrapping, continue shopping, estimate shipping. While related, these are logically separable and could be tested/released independently. For example, a store could have 'add to cart' without 'apply coupon' initially. The current bundling makes it hard to estimate work or prioritize implementation.
findings.recommendation: Decompose F04 into: F04a (Core Cart - add/view/update/remove), F04b (Discount Management - coupons and gift cards), F04c (Shipping Estimation), F04d (Gift Wrapping). Each has independent business value and implementation complexity. This enables: (1) MVP with F04a only, (2) Phase 2 adding F04b, (3) Phase 3 adding F04c/F04d. Maintains coherence through clear dependencies.

findings.finding_title: Login-Related Requirements Scattered Across Multiple Features
findings.business_story: As a QA lead, I want all login-related requirements consolidated into a single feature specification, So that I can create comprehensive test cases covering all login scenarios
findings.reasoning: Login functionality is described in REQ002 (as part of authentication), REQ052 (Login Functionality), REQ053 (Remember Me), REQ054 (Forgot Password), REQ057 (Login Error Messages), REQ060 (Login Button during checkout). These requirements are scattered across different requirement IDs and don't form a cohesive 'Login Feature' specification. A tester reading REQ052 alone might miss Remember Me or error handling requirements.
findings.recommendation: Create a consolidated 'Login Feature' specification containing: (1) Login form requirements (email/password fields, validation), (2) Remember Me checkbox behavior, (3) Password recovery flow, (4) Error message handling, (5) Security measures (brute-force protection, lockout), (6) Login success behavior. All requirements (REQ052-054, REQ057) should be consolidated under this single feature with cross-references showing where they appear in different contexts (standard login vs. checkout).

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Promotional Pricing and Discounts Visibility
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to clearly see product discounts, sale prices, and promotional offers on product listings and details, So that I can identify good deals and save money
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention 'old price for comparison (strikethrough)' (REQ008, REQ016) and discount code application (REQ043), but there is NO comprehensive feature for displaying promotional pricing and visual indicators (e.g., 'Sale' badge, percent-off labels, countdown timers for limited-time promotions). The requirements show WHAT prices are displayed but not HOW promotional pricing is visually highlighted or communicated to drive conversion. This is a critical gap for e-commerce user experience.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F27: Promotional Pricing Display' with sub-features: (1) Sale Price Highlighting (old price strikethrough, new price emphasis), (2) Discount Percentage/Amount Display, (3) 'Sale' or 'Limited Time' Badges, (4) Promotional Message Display, (5) Countdown Timer for Time-Limited Offers. This clarifies how the store communicates promotional value to customers and improves conversion optimization.

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Multi-Store and Store Administration
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to understand which store I'm shopping from and (if applicable) switch between multiple branded stores, So that I can access different product catalogs or store configurations
findings.reasoning: Requirements frequently mention 'multi-store' context and store-specific visibility settings (e.g., 'limited to specific stores', 'multi-store configurations'), but there is NO customer-facing feature described for: (1) How customers see/switch between stores, (2) Store selection UI, (3) Store-specific catalog/pricing, (4) Store locator feature. The requirements mention multi-store as an admin configuration concern but don't address the customer user experience when multiple stores exist.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F28: Multi-Store Visibility and Switching' with sub-features: (1) Store Selector/Switcher UI, (2) Store-Specific Catalog Display, (3) Store Locator Map, (4) Store-Specific Pricing/Currency Display. This is particularly important if the e-commerce platform serves multiple brands, regions, or languages. Without this feature clarity, developers may implement inconsistent multi-store UX.

findings.finding_title: Missing Feature: Customer Reward Points System
findings.business_story: As a customer, I want to earn reward points on my purchases and redeem them for discounts, So that I feel valued and incentivized to continue shopping
findings.reasoning: Multiple requirements mention 'reward points' as display elements: REQ004 mentions 'reward points earned' in order totals, REQ047 mentions reward points in order summary, and REQ002 mentions 'reward points' as accessible in 'My Account'. However, there is NO requirement describing the complete reward points feature: (1) How points are earned, (2) Point calculation rules, (3) How to redeem points, (4) Point expiration, (5) Reward tiers. The feature is implied but not explicitly specified.
findings.recommendation: Create a new feature 'F30: Loyalty and Reward Points' with sub-features: (1) Reward Points Earning (point per purchase rule), (2) Points Display (current balance in account/checkout), (3) Points Redemption (redeem as discount in checkout), (4) Reward Tier Management (if applicable). This clarifies customer expectations for earning and using rewards, improving satisfaction and retention.

findings.finding_title: Validation Requirements Are Feature-Level, Not Implementation Details
findings.business_story: As a quality manager, I want validation rules separated from feature definitions so that acceptance criteria focus on business behavior, not implementation mechanics
findings.reasoning: Multiple requirements are purely technical validation concerns (REQ055: Email validation, REQ062: Mandatory fields, REQ066: Form validation, REQ067: Email format validation, REQ068: Zip code validation). These are important for quality but shouldn't be standalone 'features'. They're implementation details of larger features (Registration, Address Entry, etc.). Current structure makes the feature list harder to read and prioritize from a business perspective.
findings.recommendation: Move validation requirements into acceptance criteria of their parent features rather than standalone requirements. Example: Move REQ067 (Email format validation) into F16 (User Registration) acceptance criteria: 'Email field validates email format before submission'. This keeps feature definitions focused on business value while maintaining validation rigor in acceptance criteria.

findings.finding_title: Payment Processing Lacks Clear Feature Boundaries
findings.business_story: As a payment architect, I want to understand the separation between manual payment entry, payment gateway integration, and payment status management, So that security and compliance requirements are clear
findings.reasoning: Payment features are scattered: REQ083-090 cover credit card information entry (manual form), REQ091 mentions secure processing and PCI compliance, REQ101 mentions payment status display. The requirements don't clearly separate: (1) Manual credit card entry (legacy, not recommended), (2) Hosted payment pages (card data never touches merchant server), (3) Payment gateway integration (Stripe, PayPal, etc.), (4) Payment status tracking. This ambiguity creates security risks if developers implement insecure payment capture.
findings.recommendation: Restructure payment features: F13 (Payment Information Entry) should explicitly state 'Manual Credit Card Entry' is legacy only and redirect to F13a (Hosted Payment Pages) for production use. Create separate feature: F32 (Payment Gateway Integration) covering Stripe, PayPal, etc. Clarify in F13 requirements that PCI compliance requires hosted payment pages or tokenization, not manual entry. This prevents security vulnerabilities.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Inventory Depletion Notification
findings.outcome_description: The system will notify the store administrator when product inventory falls below a critical threshold
findings.triggering_scenario: Stock level decreases during order processing or manual inventory adjustments
findings.reasoning: Requirements REQ030 (Quantity Selection) and REQ029 (Add to Cart) validate stock availability but have no specification of administrative outcomes. The system checks 'if inventory tracking is enabled' and validates quantities don't exceed stock (REQ040), but there is NO requirement that the system notifies administrators of low stock, out-of-stock events, or backorder status. This gaps the operational visibility needed for inventory management.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'The system shall send a low stock alert email to the store administrator when a product's stock quantity falls below a configurable threshold (e.g., 10 units), and shall notify when a product transitions to out-of-stock status.' Define which stakeholders receive notifications, threshold configurations, and alert frequency.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Reward Points Redemption and Balance Management
findings.outcome_description: The system will update customer reward points balance after order completion and redemption
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer completes order (REQ047, REQ093) or redeems reward points
findings.reasoning: REQ047 and REQ093 mention 'reward points earned for the order' displayed in the order summary, and REQ004 mentions reward points, but there is NO requirement specifying the outcome: when/how are points credited? When are they redeemable? What is the customer notification for point accumulation? F18 (Order Management) lists 'reward points' as viewable but not the outcome of earning or spending them. The glossary defines Reward Points as 'accumulating in their account and optionally redeemable' but this is not a requirement outcome.
findings.recommendation: Add requirements: (1) 'After order completion, the system shall calculate reward points earned based on order total and product-specific point values, add points to customer account, and display confirmation.' (2) 'When customer redeems reward points for payment, system shall deduct points, apply monetary value per point, reduce order total, and display remaining balance.' Include in Feature F18 sub-features.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Newsletter Subscription Confirmation
findings.outcome_description: The system will send a confirmation email when a customer subscribes to the newsletter
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer subscribes to newsletter during registration (F16, REQ122) or via footer newsletter box
findings.reasoning: REQ122 and REQ124 describe the newsletter subscription checkbox and its configuration but do not specify the outcome: Does the system send a subscription confirmation email? What is the customer notification? Is double opt-in required? No specification of: welcome email, confirmation link, unsubscribe mechanism visibility, GDPR compliance notifications. The glossary mentions 'Subscribe' but not the communication outcome. Feature F16 (Registration) includes newsletter as a sub-feature but not the subscription confirmation outcome.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'When a customer subscribes to the newsletter (during registration or via the newsletter box), the system SHALL send a subscription confirmation email containing: welcome message, unsubscribe link, frequency information, and data privacy notice for GDPR compliance.' Define single vs. double opt-in strategy.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Order Status Change Notifications for Admins
findings.outcome_description: The system will notify the administrator when an order status changes (Processing, Complete, Cancelled)
findings.triggering_scenario: Administrator manually changes order status or system auto-completes order
findings.reasoning: REQ107 describes order status tracking with four states (Pending, Processing, Complete, Cancelled) and automatic completion rules, but does not specify outcomes: Does the admin receive notification when automatically completed? When status is manually changed? What about the customer? The requirements focus on WHAT statuses exist, not on the OUTCOMES when they change. No specification of automated status change notifications, admin alerts, or workflow triggers.
findings.recommendation: Add requirements: (1) 'When order status automatically transitions (e.g., from Pending to Processing when payment is received), the system SHALL log the transition and send a notification email to the administrator.' (2) 'When an administrator manually changes order status, the system SHALL log the change with timestamp/user, send notification to administrator(s), and if status is Complete or Cancelled, send status change notification email to the customer.' Define which status changes trigger which notifications.

findings.finding_title: Missing Outcome: Gift Card Activation and Balance Notifications
findings.outcome_description: The system will send gift card delivery email and balance notifications when redemption occurs
findings.triggering_scenario: Customer purchases gift card (REQ044) or redeems gift card balance (REQ044, REQ047)
findings.reasoning: REQ044 defines gift card features: 'Virtual (delivered via email) or Physical (shipped), activated either automatically when order is completed or manually by administrator, each gift card has unique coupon code, initial value, remaining balance, recipient details, and usage history.' However, there is NO requirement specifying the outcome when a gift card is purchased: Does the recipient receive an email? When is the code sent? What is the notification content? Same for balance notifications - the glossary states 'optionally redeemable as payment' but outcomes are not specified. What happens when a gift card is redeemed? Is balance checked? Is customer notified? No.
findings.recommendation: Add requirements: (1) 'When a customer purchases a virtual gift card, the system SHALL immediately send an email to the recipient containing: gift card code, value, instructions for redemption, and personal message from purchaser.' (2) 'When a customer applies a gift card to an order, the system SHALL display remaining balance after application and send confirmation email showing deducted amount.' (3) 'When a gift card balance reaches zero, the system SHALL notify the customer that the gift card is depleted.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Email Validation and Double-Entry Confirmation
findings.scenario_text: Given 'Force entering email twice' is enabled in Customer settings during registration, When a customer enters email 'john@example.com' in first field and 'john@example.co' (typo) in confirmation field, Then the system should display validation error 'Email addresses do not match' before form submission, blocking account creation; customer must correct the mismatch before registration succeeds
findings.reasoning: REQ055 mentions email validation and optional double-entry requirement, but no scenario explicitly tests the mismatch detection and error handling when two email entries don't match.
findings.recommendation: Add edge case scenario testing email double-entry validation (both typos and intentional mismatches). Test that email uniqueness is checked only after double-entry validation passes. Test case sensitivity in email matching.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Wishlist Quantity Updates and Cart Transfers
findings.scenario_text: Given a customer has product 'Monitor' in wishlist with quantity 1, When customer updates wishlist quantity to 3 and clicks 'Update wishlist', Then wishlist reflects quantity 3; When customer then clicks 'Add to cart' (with 'Move items' configured), Then all 3 units transfer to shopping cart, wishlist is cleared of this item, and cart displays Monitor quantity 3 ready for checkout
findings.reasoning: REQ003, REQ129, REQ127 describe wishlist management and transfer to cart, but no comprehensive scenario tests the quantity update flow and the move-vs-copy behavior when transferring items to cart.
findings.recommendation: Add scenario testing wishlist quantity updates, testing both 'move' and 'copy' modes when adding items to cart. Test that quantity in cart matches updated wishlist quantity. Test that wishlist quantity changes don't affect cart until explicit 'Add to cart' action.

findings.finding_title: Missing Scenario for Reward Points Redemption at Checkout
findings.scenario_text: Given a registered customer has accumulated 500 reward points (each point = $0.01 redemption value = $5.00 credit), and cart total is $50, When customer reaches cart/checkout and sees reward points option showing 'You can apply up to 500 points ($5.00 credit)', and chooses to redeem 300 points ($3.00), Then order total reduces from $50.00 to $47.00, customer's account is debited 300 points, and invoice shows 'Reward Points Applied: -$3.00'
findings.reasoning: Requirements mention reward points (REQ004, REQ047, REQ093) as order totals component, but no scenario explicitly tests the redemption flow, points application to reduce order total, and account debit process.
findings.recommendation: Add scenario testing reward points redemption at cart/checkout. Test partial redemption (not all points used). Test insufficient points (customer tries to redeem more than available). Test points earned vs. redeemed in same order.

findings.finding_title: Assumed Behavior: Free Shipping Threshold Interaction with Tiered Shipping
findings.scenario: Store offers: 'Free shipping for orders over $100', 'Ground Shipping $10 for orders under $100', and 'Next Day Air $25'. Customer's subtotal is $99, adding $10 shipping brings total to $109 (now qualifies for free shipping), but the fee is already applied.
findings.predicted_outcome: System recalculates and shows $99 subtotal + $0 shipping = $99 total (free shipping threshold triggered by moving into a higher price tier)
findings.realistic_alternative: System shows $99 subtotal + $10 shipping = $109 total (free shipping calculation does not re-evaluate after shipping cost is added)
findings.absurd_alternative: System applies both free shipping AND charges $10, resulting in $99 subtotal - $0 + $10 = $109 total with confusing math
findings.reasoning: REQ046 describes shipping estimation and fee display, REQ070-REQ076 cover shipping method selection, but the requirements do NOT address: (1) the order of calculation (does free shipping threshold apply before or after computing base shipping cost?), (2) whether free shipping is a separate rule or a special shipping method, (3) how tiered rules (cost-based free shipping) interact with multiple shipping options, (4) whether the system automatically applies the best-price option or requires customer selection. The glossary and requirements never mention free shipping as a feature, only that it can be configured per product.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit requirement: 'Free shipping rules (configured via Shipping → Shipping methods → Free shipping option or via discounts) SHALL be evaluated as follows: (1) Calculate base shipping cost based on selected method and weight/destination. (2) Check if order subtotal meets free shipping threshold defined in [configuration location]. (3) If threshold met, apply free shipping by setting shipping cost to $0.00 and display message "Free shipping applied!". (4) If multiple shipping methods qualify for free shipping, the system SHALL allow customer to select any free option, or pre-select the fastest/cheapest option.' Define the calculation order and customer experience.

findings.finding_title: Vague Timing: 'Soon', 'Immediately'
findings.text_excerpt: The system uses the 'ContactUs.EmailAMessage' message template. After successful submission, a confirmation message is displayed to the customer. The inquiry is dispatched as an email to the configured store contact email address.
findings.interpretation_b: 'Dispatched as email' = queued for eventual sending (could be minutes/hours later)
findings.reasoning: REQ139 says 'email notification is queued in Message Queue and sent by scheduled task'—but 'dispatched' is ambiguous. Does customer see 'Email sent immediately' confirmation or 'Email queued for sending'? SLA not specified. Customer expects email 'immediately' but system may queue it.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'dispatched' with precise timing: 'The inquiry email is queued in the Message Queue and sent by scheduled task within 5 minutes' or 'The email is sent immediately via SMTP'

findings.finding_title: Vague Quantifier: 'All Configured' Attributes - Scope Unclear
findings.text_excerpt: REQ028: 'specification attributes table (filterable attributes like screen size, CPU type displayed in a structured table), product attributes (dropdowns, radio buttons, checkboxes, color squares for variations like size/color with price/weight adjustments)'
findings.interpretation_a: ALL product attributes must support price adjustments
findings.interpretation_b: SOME attributes may support price adjustments; others may not
findings.reasoning: Requirements say 'with price/weight adjustments' but don't clearly state whether this is mandatory for all attributes or optional. Can an attribute lack a price adjustment? What are the business rules?
findings.recommendation: Specify: 'Product attributes MAY optionally include price and weight adjustments per attribute value' OR 'All product attributes MUST support configurable price/weight adjustments'

findings.finding_title: Vague Quantifier: 'Optional' Display Settings Without Clear Defaults
findings.text_excerpt: REQ008: 'The catalog pages panel in settings allows configuring default view mode (grid or list), product image display, and whether to show short descriptions'. REQ028: 'The product details page displays...specification attributes table (filterable attributes like screen size, CPU type displayed in a structured table)'
findings.interpretation_a: All optional settings default to 'ON' if admin doesn't configure
findings.interpretation_b: All optional settings default to 'OFF' if admin doesn't configure
findings.reasoning: Requirements say 'configurable' and 'optional' but don't specify DEFAULT values. Is product image display ON or OFF by default? Does category show short descriptions by default? Admin might miss configuration, leaving feature invisible.
findings.recommendation: Specify for each configurable setting: '(enabled by default)' or '(disabled by default)'. Example: 'Product image display (enabled by default)' and 'Show short description (disabled by default)'

findings.finding_title: Inconsistency: Product Attribute Combinations vs SKU Replacement
findings.text_excerpt: REQ003: 'When a product with variants is added, all chosen variants are included...appears once with updated quantity'. REQ038: 'For products with attribute combinations, each combination can have its own unique SKU that replaces the base product SKU when that combination is selected.'
findings.interpretation_a: Adding product with variants (SKU combo A) then same product with different variants (SKU combo B) should create separate wishlist items
findings.interpretation_b: Adding same product with different variants increments the same wishlist item's quantity
findings.reasoning: REQ003 says 'same product multiple times...appears once with updated quantity' (combining quantities) but REQ038 suggests each SKU combination is distinct. If customer adds Red-Size-M then Blue-Size-L, are these 1 item (qty 2) or 2 items in wishlist?
findings.recommendation: Clarify: 'When a product with different attribute combinations is added to wishlist/cart, create a separate line item per unique combination. Qty increments only for identical combinations'

findings.finding_title: Undefined Default Behavior: Pre-selected vs Defaulted
findings.text_excerpt: REQ072: 'The shipping method step in checkout pre-selects the first available shipping option by default using a radio button selection'. REQ079: 'The payment method step pre-selects the first available payment method using a radio button'.
findings.interpretation_a: First in list = first added to admin = always same choice
findings.interpretation_b: First available = cheapest/fastest shipping = could change per order
findings.reasoning: What defines 'first available'? Is it first in admin's configured order? Lowest cost? Fastest? Cheapest first creates poor UX if customer wants fast shipping but default is slow ground. Different interpretations lead to different UX.
findings.recommendation: Specify: 'The system SHALL pre-select the least-expensive shipping method by default, OR the first method as configured by administrator in display order, with clear indication to customer'

findings.finding_title: Inconsistency: Email Notification Rules Unclear
findings.text_excerpt: REQ020: 'notify store owner of new reviews (configurable)'. REQ139: 'Notify about new blog comments' is enabled/disabled. REQ140: 'inquiry is dispatched as an email to the configured store contact email address'.
findings.interpretation_a: All notifications are configurable per event type
findings.interpretation_b: Some notifications are mandatory; others are optional
findings.reasoning: REQ020 says review notifications are 'configurable', REQ139 says blog comments notifications 'can be enabled', but REQ140 doesn't mention if contact form notifications are configurable. Are ALL email notifications opt-in? Or is contact form mandatory?
findings.recommendation: Document all notification scenarios: 'Order placed (mandatory), Order paid (configurable: default ON), New review (configurable: default ON), Contact form (mandatory), Blog comment (configurable: default ON)'

findings.finding_title: Missing Data Validation: Product Attribute Validation Rules Incomplete
findings.what_is_missing: REQ030 mentions product attributes but does NOT specify: (1) Which attributes are required vs. optional, (2) Validation for attribute values (e.g., Size limited to XS/S/M/L/XL), (3) Conditional attributes (show color only if product is apparel), (4) Attribute format validation (date attributes, numeric ranges)
findings.where_expected: REQ030 Product Attributes or detailed specification of attribute types
findings.impact: Customers submit invalid attribute combinations; system accepts malformed data; order fulfillment impossible (e.g., order for t-shirt size 'purple'). Data quality degraded.
findings.reasoning: Requirements describe attribute collection but not validation rules. Different product types need different validation; specification is incomplete.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'Product attributes SHALL support predefined validation types: (1) Dropdown List (e.g., Size: XS, S, M, L, XL), (2) Text Input with optional length limit, (3) Numeric Range (e.g., Screen Diagonal: 10-100 inches), (4) Date selector. Admin SHALL be able to mark attributes as required or optional per product. Conditional display rules: show attribute only if another attribute has specific value (e.g., show "Monogram Text" only if "Add Monogram" = Yes)'

findings.finding_title: Missing CRUD Specification: Product Images (Create/Read/Update/Delete)
findings.what_is_missing: Requirements display product images (REQ009, REQ028, REQ034) but do NOT specify admin capability to: (1) Upload multiple images per product, (2) Set primary image, (3) Organize image sequence, (4) Delete images, (5) Image format/size validation, (6) Thumbnail generation
findings.where_expected: Product Management admin feature
findings.impact: Admins cannot manage product images; no image upload workflow defined; image quality/size issues unspecified; no thumbnail support for gallery
findings.reasoning: Customer-facing display of images specified but not the admin CRUD operations to manage images. Incomplete product management feature.
findings.recommendation: Add requirement: 'Admin product editor SHALL support image management: (1) Upload multiple images (JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP). (2) Validate image dimensions (min 300x300, max 5000x5000), file size (max 10MB). (3) Auto-generate thumbnail (300x300) and medium (800x800) versions. (4) Drag-drop reorder images; set first image as primary. (5) Delete individual images (confirm not in use by variant). (6) View image in modal with zoom. (7) Bulk image upload from ZIP file. (8) Image alt-text edit for accessibility.'

findings.finding_title: Missing Error Handling: Shipping Address Validation
findings.what_is_missing: REQ062-064 collect shipping address but do NOT specify: (1) Postal code format validation (US ZIP vs UK postcode), (2) Country-dependent field requirements (some countries have no state), (3) Address verification (USPS validation for US addresses), (4) What happens if address is invalid or incomplete
findings.where_expected: REQ062-064 Billing/Shipping Address or address validation sub-feature
findings.impact: Invalid addresses accepted; shipping fails; customer charged for failed delivery; orders stuck. Address country doesn't match format (e.g., US ZIP format for UK).
